Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive view of the codebase. Let me compile the detailed summary for the user.

## COMPREHENSIVE DECK-GENERATOR PROJECT ANALYSIS

### 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW
This is a **presentation deck generator** built with Vite (bundler) and vanilla JavaScript, designed for creating, editing, and exporting professional presentations with multiple design themes. The tool generates slides from JSON content files, applies design tokens (color palettes and typography), renders them in the browser for editing, and exports as PNG, PDF, and PowerPoint (PPTX) files.

**Tech Stack:**
- Frontend: Vanilla JS (ES modules), HTML/CSS, Three.js (WebGL)
- Build: Vite 6.0.0
- Rendering/Export: html2canvas, Playwright (headless Chrome)
- Media: sharp (PNG optimization), pdfkit, pptxgenjs
- Compression: pako (deflate/inflate)

---

### 2. ARCHITECTURE

#### **Entry Point: index.html**
Located at `/Users/stugrey/Documents/Student Voice ICD/Conferences:Webinars/Teesside Keynote/deck-generator/index.html`

- Loads 9 pre-built token CSS files (design themes): monzo, dia, beyond-trends, artsy, ace-tate, glide, space10, superlist, wetransfer
- Google Fonts preload for 9 typography families
- Two main containers: `#controls` (UI panel) and `#deck` (slide rendering)
- Loads `/src/main.js` as module entry point

#### **Core Source Files: `/src/`**

**main.js** (954 lines) — Application orchestrator
- Initializes config/content from JSON, manages UI lifecycle
- Renders controls panel (palette/typography/shapes/pinstripes/noise selectors + Save/Export buttons)
- Implements slide pagination (5 slides per page)
- Handles slide editing (collapsible per-slide edit panels with live preview)
- Manages slide operations: duplicate, export PNG, edit inline
- Implements full export workflow (16:9 + 4:3 aspect ratios)

**layouts.js** (254 lines) — Layout template functions
- 10 layout templates organized by slide type:
  - **Cover** (2): `cover-bold-full` (Monzo-inspired, bold title), `cover-dark-minimal` (Glide-inspired, minimal)
  - **Section Divider** (2): `divider-color-burst`, `divider-dark-cinematic`
  - **Content** (2): `content-split-cards` (50/50 text + cards), `content-wide-visual` (30/70 text + grid)
  - **Data** (2): `data-big-numbers` (4 stat blocks), `data-full-bleed` (full-width stats)
  - **Statement** (2): `statement-centered` (Superlist), `statement-editorial` (WeTransfer)
- All layouts use CSS custom properties (tokens) — zero hard-coded colors/fonts
- Returns HTML strings; support regex-based text highlighting

**shapes.js** (214 lines) — Visual overlay generators
- **Simple shapes** (return HTML string):
  - `shapesNone()` — no overlay
  - `shapesPills()` — WeTransfer-style capsules
  - `shapesBlobs()` — Ace & Tate organic SVG blobs
  - `shapesBlocks()` — Artsy color rectangles
  - `shapesGoldenRect()` — DIA geometric SVG curves
- **WebGL shapes** (return `{html, init}` object):
  - 7 variants of water droplets with different materials: base, tinted (mint/coral), crude oil (iridescent), mercury (liquid metal), frosted (blurred glass), ice (crystalline)
  - Each captures slide content as texture and refracts it through metaball field

**shapes-droplets.js** (860+ lines) — Advanced WebGL material system
- Implements Three.js-based water physics with:
  - Metaball field (Gaussian kernel) for organic droplet shapes
  - Refraction through meniscus (curved water-air interface)
  - Chromatic aberration (wavelength-dependent bending)
  - Fresnel reflection (angle-dependent reflectivity)
  - Specular highlights (mirror + broad lobe)
  - Environment map lighting (HDRP from `/public/env-theater.hdr`)
- **6 shader variants**:
  1. Clear water (all optics, Fresnel R₀=0.02)
  2. Tinted water (Beer-Lambert absorption by colour)
  3. Crude oil (dark opaque, thin-film iridescence, R₀=0.04)
  4. Mercury (liquid metal, spectral reflectance 0.78-0.85 per channel, completely opaque)
  5. Frosted glass (37-tap Gaussian blur, scattering veil)
  6. Frozen ice (blue tint, internal cracks — not fully shown in excerpt)
- Droplet positions generated via seeded Mulberry32 PRNG (3–5 puddle groups + scattered drops, up to 96 total)
- Captures slide HTML as texture via html2canvas, applies refraction in real-time

**style.css** (1000+ lines) — Layout, controls, and typography
- Dark theme for editor UI (#1a1a1a background, cyan accents #5DD4E0)
- Responsive controls panel (sticky, collapsible, dark monospace styling)
- Pagination UI with prev/next buttons
- Slide container: 16:9 aspect ratio (via CSS custom property `--export-aspect`, overridable to 4:3)
- Detailed layout-specific styles for each of the 10 layouts:
  - Cover slides: title centering, pill-shaped date badges
  - Content slides: two-column layout, card grids, evidence cards
  - Data slides: stat blocks with adaptive text color/sizing (JS-driven)
  - Statement slides: centered large text with highlight spans
- Adaptive stat block contrast system (luminance-based text color selection)
- Edit panel styling (inline form grid, label/input pairs)

---

### 3. CONTENT & CONFIGURATION STRUCTURE

#### **content.json** (current, 64 slides, 2.5KB excerpt → ~43KB total)
Structure for each slide:
```json
{
  "type": "cover|section-divider|content|data|statement",
  "section": "section name",
  "title/heading": "text",
  "subtitle/body": "text",
  "highlight": "text to highlight (statement slides)",
  "attribution": "optional",
  "points": [{"title": "...", "description": "..."}, ...],  // content slides
  "stats": [{"value": "...", "label": "...", "year": "..."}, ...],  // data slides
  "author": "Dr Stuart Grey",
  "badge": "event/series",
  "date": "April 15th 2026"
}
```

**Current keynote topic:** "Who Will Control the AI Infrastructure of Higher Education?" — 64-slide presentation exploring:
- Section 1: From tool to substrate (AI moving from visible tools → invisible infrastructure)
- Section 2: The build-out (speculative capital creating real capacity)
- Section 3: Who sets the defaults? (market concentration, agenda-setting)
- Section 4: What dependence does (pedagogy, labour, data, inequality)
- Section 5: The lifecycle of capture (generosity → squeeze → lock-in)
- Section 6: A different strategy (engaged stewardship)
- Section 7: What the next decade looks like (two futures)

#### **config.json** (current)
```json
{
  "palette": "monzo",               // one of 9 color themes
  "typography": "monzo",            // one of 9 typeface sets
  "shapes": "none",                 // global default shape type
  "pinstripes": "diagonal",         // texture overlay: none|vertical|horizontal|diagonal
  "noise": 5,                       // noise opacity 0-30%
  "layouts": {                      // per-slide-type layout choice
    "cover": "cover-bold-full",
    "section-divider": "divider-color-burst",
    "content": "content-split-cards",
    "data": "data-big-numbers",
    "statement": "statement-centered"
  },
  "slideShapes": {                  // per-slide overrides (0-indexed)
    "0": "droplets",
    "3": "droplets",
    "4": "droplets-mercury",
    "5": "droplets-oil"
  }
}
```

---

### 4. DESIGN SYSTEM — TOKENS

**9 Pre-built Design Palettes** in `/tokens/`, each defining:
- **Palette** (primary/secondary/dark/accent colors, text colors)
- **Typography** (display/body fonts, sizes, weights, spacing, transforms)

**Example: Monzo theme** (`/tokens/monzo.css`)
- Colors: hot coral (#FF5C57), mint (#C8F0D8), navy (#1B2A3D)
- Fonts: Plus Jakarta Sans (display, 800wt), Inter (body, 400wt)
- Headings: 9rem max, 0.95 line-height, -0.03em tracking (tight)

**Example: WeTransfer theme** (`/tokens/wetransfer.css`)
- Colors: black (#000000), pastels (lavender #C8A8E0, lime #C8E050, periwinkle #88A8E8)
- Fonts: Playfair Display (serif, 900wt), Source Serif 4 (body)
- Headings: 6.5rem, 1.1 line-height (more open than Monzo)

Other themes: DIA (light serif, all-caps), Beyond Trends (soft rounded serif), Artsy (geometric sans), Ace & Tate (Didone serif), Glide (geometric sans, tight), SPACE10 (light clean sans), Superlist (bold geometric sans)

---

### 5. WORKFLOW & FEATURES

#### **Interactive Controls Panel**
Users can:
1. **Choose global design**: Palette (9), Typography (9), Shapes (11), Pinstripes (4), Noise (0-30%)
2. **Choose per-slide-type layouts**: 2 options each for cover, divider, content, data, statement
3. **Per-slide shape override**: Dropdown to apply different shape to individual slides
4. **Randomise**: One-click random theme combo (palette + typography + shapes + pinstripes + noise + layouts)
5. **Edit slide content**: Inline editor for all text fields and nested arrays (points, stats)
6. **Pagination**: 5 slides per page, prev/next navigation
7. **Save**: Saves content.json + config.json to disk with timestamped backups
8. **Export**: All slides to PNG, PDF, PPTX (both 16:9 and 4:3)

#### **Slide Operations** (per-slide buttons)
- **Edit**: Toggle collapsible inline editor
- **Duplicate**: Clone slide (preserves slide-specific shape overrides with index shifting)
- **Export PNG**: Download single slide as PNG

#### **Export Workflow** (`exportAllSlides()`)
1. Iterate through all slides across all pages
2. Render each slide in both 16:9 and 4:3 aspect ratios
3. Capture via html2canvas at 3840px width (2x device pixel ratio)
4. POST to `/api/save-slide` endpoint per slide
5. Optimize PNGs via sharp (lossless compression, level 9)
6. Build PPTX and PDF from PNG files via POST to `/api/build-deck`
7. Display results (64 slides exported, PPTX + PDF created)

#### **Save System**
- Two endpoints: `/api/save-content` (legacy), `/api/save` (combined)
- Auto-backs up content.json and config.json with timestamp (e.g., `content_2026-03-30T10-37-12.json`)
- Client-side dirty state tracking (Save button enabled only when changes made)

---

### 6. BUILD & DEPLOYMENT

#### **Vite Configuration** (`vite.config.js`)
- **Dev server** opens browser automatically
- **Custom middleware** for three key endpoints:
  - `/api/save`: Accepts JSON (content + config), backs up originals, writes new versions
  - `/api/save-slide`: Accepts base64 PNG data, saves to `export/{subdir}/` (16x9 or 4x3)
  - `/api/build-deck`: Iterates over exported PNGs, builds PPTX and PDF files

**PPTX/PDF generation details:**
- **PPTX**: Custom layout (16:9 = 13.333"×7.5", 4:3 = 10"×7.5"), each slide = one image
- **PDF**: PDFKit with matching dimensions, one slide per page
- Both stored in `/export/{16x9|4x3}/presentation.{pptx|pdf}`

#### **npm Scripts**
```
npm run dev        → Start Vite dev server (localhost:5173), auto-open browser
npm run build      → Vite production build (not used for this workflow)
npm run preview    → Preview production build
npm run export     → Node script using Playwright to render slides to PNG
```

**Export script** (`scripts/export.js`):
- Uses Playwright chromium headless browser
- Navigates to dev server, waits for fonts + stabilization
- Selects all `.slide` elements, screenshots each to output directory
- Configurable: `--out`, `--width`, `--url`

---

### 7. INTERACTIVE FEATURES

#### **Real-time Editing**
- Inline edit forms for slide content (title, heading, body, stats, points, etc.)
- Changes immediately re-render the slide (no page reload)
- Edit panel collapsible per-slide
- Nested arrays (points in content slides, stats in data slides) fully editable

#### **Theme Mixing**
- Palette + Typography independently selectable (e.g., Monzo colors + DIA typography)
- Pinstripes overlay (vertical/horizontal/diagonal lines via CSS or canvas texture)
- Noise texture (procedurally generated grayscale noise, opacity 0-30%)
- Per-slide shape overrides (can use different material for each slide while global default applies to rest)

#### **WebGL Interactivity** (for droplet shapes)
- Real-time 3D rendering on each slide containing droplets
- Captures slide content as texture (via html2canvas before WebGL init)
- Refracts texture through metaball field with physics-based materials
- No user interaction controls; purely visual overlays

#### **Adaptive Stat Sizing**
- `fitStatSizes()`: Measures longest stat value text, scales all blocks to match
- `fixStatBlockContrast()`: Analyzes stat block background luminance, selects black or white text adaptively
- Handles contrast borders if block color is too close to slide background

#### **Aspect Ratio Toggle**
- CSS custom property `--export-aspect` overridable via JS (default 16:9)
- 4:3 export involves full re-render of all droplets to capture content at new aspect ratio
- Fallback plain shape rendering (pills, blobs, blocks, golden-rect) works at any aspect

---

### 8. DATA PERSISTENCE & BACKUPS

All changes backed up with timestamps:
- Content changes: `content_YYYY-MM-DDTHH-mm-ss.json`
- Config changes: `config_YYYY-MM-DDTHH-mm-ss.json`
- Originals: `content.json`, `config.json` (current versions)
- Export folder: `export/{16x9|4x3}/slide-NN-{type}.png`, `.pptx`, `.pdf`

**Current backup count**: 17+ timestamped versions visible (March 30, 2026)

---

### 9. KEY TECHNICAL INNOVATIONS

1. **Physics-based WebGL droplets**: Real refraction, chromatic aberration, Fresnel, specular reflection, environment mapping — 6 distinct material presets
2. **Seeded procedural droplet generation**: Deterministic per-slide (Mulberry32 PRNG), replicable layouts
3. **Texture capture for refraction**: html2canvas snapshot embedded as WebGL content texture
4. **Adaptive typography**: Stat values auto-scale to fit blocks; text color adapts to background luminance
5. **Design token system**: CSS custom properties enable swapping entire visual identity without code changes
6. **Dual-format export**: 16:9 and 4:3 renders in single workflow
7. **Live inline editing with preview**: No modal dialogs, full re-render on keystroke
8. **Per-element shape overrides**: Can apply different materials to individual slides while maintaining global defaults

---

### 10. PRESENTATION CONTENT SUMMARY

The current keynote ("Who Will Control the AI Infrastructure of Higher Education?") uses all 10 layout types across 64 slides:
- **1 cover slide** with speaker/date badge
- **7 section dividers** (one intro section + 6 main sections)
- **40+ content/data slides** mixing two-column evidence cards, stat blocks, and text blocks
- **9+ statement slides** emphasizing key themes (Transparency, Distortion, Extraction, Control, etc.)

Themes explored:
- AI as infrastructure (not tool)
- Market concentration & dependency
- Agenda-setting and mission drift
- Pedagogical and labour impacts
- Lock-in through convenience
- Strategic alternatives (stewardship, collaboration, data governance)
- Two possible futures (capability vs. rent extraction)

---

### CONCLUSION

This is a **sophisticated presentation tool** combining:
- Modular layout + shape system (10 layouts × 11 shape types = 110 unique combinations)
- 9 design themes (palette + typography) = ~1000 total theme variations
- Real-time editing with live preview
- Enterprise-grade export (PNG + PDF + PPTX, both 16:9 and 4:3)
- Advanced WebGL material effects (water, oil, mercury, frosted glass)
- Token-driven design (fully themeable via CSS custom properties)
- Automatic backup and versioning
- Procedurally generated organic elements (droplet positions, noise textures)

The codebase is well-organized, modular, and deeply customizable. Every visual aspect can be modified through config.json or live controls without touching code.

1	import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
2	import fs from 'fs';
3	import path from 'path';
4	import sharp from 'sharp';
5	
6	/** Helper: backup a file with a timestamp, then write new data */
7	function backupAndWrite(filePath, data, ts) {
8	  const ext = path.extname(filePath);
9	  const base = path.basename(filePath, ext);
10	  const dir = path.dirname(filePath);
11	  const backupPath = path.join(dir, `${base}_${ts}${ext}`);
12	  if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
13	    fs.copyFileSync(filePath, backupPath);
14	  }
15	  fs.writeFileSync(filePath, JSON.stringify(data, null, 2) + '\n');
16	  return path.basename(backupPath);
17	}
18	
19	/** Vite plugin: save content.json + config.json via POST /api/save */
20	function savePlugin() {
21	  return {
22	    name: 'deck-save',
23	    configureServer(server) {
24	      // Keep the old endpoint working
25	      server.middlewares.use('/api/save-content', async (req, res) => {
26	        if (req.method !== 'POST') { res.statusCode = 405; res.end('Method not allowed'); return; }
27	        let body = ''; for await (const chunk of req) body += chunk;
28	        try {
29	          const json = JSON.parse(body);
30	          const ts = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-').slice(0, 19);
31	          const backup = backupAndWrite(path.resolve('content.json'), json, ts);
32	          res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');
33	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, backup }));
34	        } catch (e) { res.statusCode = 400; res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: e.message })); }
35	      });
36	
37	      // Save a PNG slide image to disk
38	      server.middlewares.use('/api/save-slide', async (req, res) => {
39	        if (req.method !== 'POST') { res.statusCode = 405; res.end('Method not allowed'); return; }
40	        const chunks = [];
41	        for await (const chunk of req) chunks.push(chunk);
42	        const body = Buffer.concat(chunks);
43	        try {
44	          // Expect JSON with { filename, dataUrl }
45	          const { filename, dataUrl, subdir } = JSON.parse(body.toString());
46	          const exportDir = subdir
47	            ? path.resolve('export', subdir)
48	            : path.resolve('export');
49	          if (!fs.existsSync(exportDir)) fs.mkdirSync(exportDir, { recursive: true });
50	          const base64 = dataUrl.replace(/^data:image\/png;base64,/, '');
51	          const rawBuf = Buffer.from(base64, 'base64');
52	          const filePath = path.join(exportDir, filename);
53	
54	          // Lossless PNG optimization via sharp — better compression, same pixels
55	          const optimized = await sharp(rawBuf)
56	            .png({ compressionLevel: 9, adaptiveFiltering: true })
57	            .toBuffer();
58	
59	          const saved = Math.round((1 - optimized.length / rawBuf.length) * 100);
60	          fs.writeFileSync(filePath, optimized);
61	          res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');
62	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, path: `export/${filename}`, saved: `${saved}%` }));
63	        } catch (e) { res.statusCode = 400; res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: e.message })); }
64	      });
65	
66	      // Build PPTX and PDF from exported PNGs
67	      server.middlewares.use('/api/build-deck', async (req, res) => {
68	        if (req.method !== 'POST') { res.statusCode = 405; res.end('Method not allowed'); return; }
69	        try {
70	          const PptxGenJS = (await import('pptxgenjs')).default;
71	          const PDFDocument = (await import('pdfkit')).default;
72	
73	          async function buildDeck(dir, widthIn, heightIn, label) {
74	            const pngs = fs.readdirSync(dir)
75	              .filter(f => f.endsWith('.png') && f.startsWith('slide-'))
76	              .sort();
77	            if (pngs.length === 0) return null;
78	
79	            // ── PPTX ──
80	            const pptx = new PptxGenJS();
81	            pptx.defineLayout({ name: 'CUSTOM', width: widthIn, height: heightIn });
82	            pptx.layout = 'CUSTOM';
83	
84	            for (const png of pngs) {
85	              const slide = pptx.addSlide();
86	              const b64 = fs.readFileSync(path.join(dir, png)).toString('base64');
87	              slide.addImage({
88	                data: `image/png;base64,${b64}`,
89	                x: 0, y: 0, w: '100%', h: '100%',
90	              });
91	            }
92	            await pptx.writeFile({ fileName: path.join(dir, 'presentation.pptx') });
93	
94	            // ── PDF ──
95	            const pageW = widthIn * 72;
96	            const pageH = heightIn * 72;
97	            await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
98	              const doc = new PDFDocument({ size: [pageW, pageH], autoFirstPage: false, margin: 0 });
99	              const stream = fs.createWriteStream(path.join(dir, 'presentation.pdf'));
100	              doc.pipe(stream);
101	              for (const png of pngs) {
102	                doc.addPage({ size: [pageW, pageH], margin: 0 });
103	                doc.image(path.join(dir, png), 0, 0, { width: pageW, height: pageH });
104	              }
105	              doc.end();
106	              stream.on('finish', resolve);
107	              stream.on('error', reject);
108	            });
109	
110	            return pngs.length;
111	          }
112	
113	          const exportDir = path.resolve('export');
114	          const dir16x9 = path.join(exportDir, '16x9');
115	          const dir4x3 = path.join(exportDir, '4x3');
116	          const results = {};
117	
118	          // 16:9 — 13.333 × 7.5 inches
119	          if (fs.existsSync(dir16x9)) {
120	            const count16 = await buildDeck(dir16x9, 13.333, 7.5, '16:9');
121	            if (count16) results['16:9'] = count16;
122	          }
123	
124	          // 4:3 — 10 × 7.5 inches
125	          if (fs.existsSync(dir4x3)) {
126	            const count43 = await buildDeck(dir4x3, 10, 7.5, '4:3');
127	            if (count43) results['4:3'] = count43;
128	          }
129	
130	          res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');
131	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, results }));
132	        } catch (e) {
133	          res.statusCode = 500;
134	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: e.message }));
135	        }
136	      });
137	
138	      // New combined endpoint
139	      server.middlewares.use('/api/save', async (req, res) => {
140	        if (req.method !== 'POST') { res.statusCode = 405; res.end('Method not allowed'); return; }
141	        let body = ''; for await (const chunk of req) body += chunk;
142	        try {
143	          const { content, config } = JSON.parse(body);
144	          const ts = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-').slice(0, 19);
145	          const backups = [];
146	          if (content) backups.push(backupAndWrite(path.resolve('content.json'), content, ts));
147	          if (config)  backups.push(backupAndWrite(path.resolve('config.json'), config, ts));
148	          res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');
149	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, backups }));
150	        } catch (e) { res.statusCode = 400; res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: e.message })); }
151	      });
152	    },
153	  };
154	}
155	
156	export default defineConfig({
157	  root: '.',
158	  server: {
159	    open: true,
160	  },
161	  plugins: [savePlugin()],
162	});
163
```

100	/** Navigate to a page, re-render, and scroll */
101	function goToPage(page, direction) {
102	  setPage(page);
103	  renderDeck();
104	  renderPagination();
105	  if (direction === 'forward') {
106	    document.getElementById('deck').scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' });

112	/** Generate a small noise texture, return data URL */
113	let noiseDataURL = '';
114	function generateNoiseTexture() {
115	  const size = 512;
116	  const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
117	  canvas.width = size;
118	  canvas.height = size;
119	  const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
120	  const img = ctx.createImageData(size, size);
121	  for (let i = 0; i < img.data.length; i += 4) {
122	    const v = Math.floor(Math.random() * 255);
123	    img.data[i] = v;
124	    img.data[i + 1] = v;
125	    img.data[i + 2] = v;
126	    img.data[i + 3] = 255;
127	  }
128	  ctx.putImageData(img, 0, 0);
129	  noiseDataURL = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
130	}
131	
132	async function init() {
133	  generateNoiseTexture();

135	  const [config, content] = await Promise.all([
136	    fetch(CONFIG_URL).then(r => r.json()),
137	    fetch(CONTENT_URL).then(r => r.json()),
138	  ]);
139	
140	  // Initialise per-slide shapes map if not present
141	  if (!config.slideShapes) config.slideShapes = {};
142	
143	  currentConfig = config;
144	  currentContent = content;
145	
146	  // Clamp restored page in case content changed
147	  const maxPage = Math.max(0, Math.ceil(content.slides.length / SLIDES_PER_PAGE) - 1);
148	  if (currentPage > maxPage) setPage(maxPage);
149	
150	  renderControls();
151	  renderDeck();
152	}

185	    </div>

187	    <div class="ctrl-body" ${collapsed ? 'style="display:none"' : ''}>
188	      <div class="ctrl-row">

201	        <div class="ctrl-group">

213	      </div>

215	        <button id="btn-randomise" class="ctrl-btn">Randomise</button>
216	      </div>
217	    </div>
218	  `;

220	  // Pagination controls (injected after the layout row)

301	/** Build pagination HTML and wire up listeners */
302	function createPaginationNav(id) {
303	  const totalSlides = currentContent ? currentContent.slides.length : 0;
304	  const totalPages = Math.ceil(totalSlides / SLIDES_PER_PAGE);
305	
306	  const nav = document.createElement('div');
307	  nav.id = id;
308	  nav.className = 'pagination';
309	
310	  const start = currentPage * SLIDES_PER_PAGE + 1;
311	  const end = Math.min(start + SLIDES_PER_PAGE - 1, totalSlides);
312	
313	  nav.innerHTML = `
314	    <button class="pg-btn pg-prev" ${currentPage === 0 ? 'disabled' : ''}>&larr; Prev</button>
315	    <span class="pg-info">Slides ${start}–${end} of ${totalSlides}</span>
316	    <button class="pg-btn pg-next" ${currentPage >= totalPages - 1 ? 'disabled' : ''}>Next &rarr;</button>
317	  `;
318	
319	  nav.querySelector('.pg-prev').addEventListener('click', () => {
320	    if (currentPage > 0) goToPage(currentPage - 1, 'back');
321	  });
322	  nav.querySelector('.pg-next').addEventListener('click', () => {
323	    if (currentPage < totalPages - 1) goToPage(currentPage + 1, 'forward');
324	  });
325	
326	  return nav;
327	}

329	/** Render pagination controls above and below the deck */
330	function renderPagination() {
331	  const totalSlides = currentContent ? currentContent.slides.length : 0;
332	  const totalPages = Math.ceil(totalSlides / SLIDES_PER_PAGE);
333	
334	  // Remove existing pagination elements
335	  document.getElementById('pagination-top')?.remove();
336	  document.getElementById('pagination-bottom')?.remove();
337	
338	  if (totalPages <= 1) return;
339	
340	  // Top pagination (after controls)
341	  document.getElementById('controls').after(createPaginationNav('pagination-top'));
342	
343	  // Bottom pagination (after deck)
344	  document.getElementById('deck').after(createPaginationNav('pagination-bottom'));
345	}

347	/** Generate a pinstripe tile as a data URL for html2canvas export */
348	function generatePinstripeTexture(direction, color) {
349	  const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
350	  const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
351	
352	  if (direction === 'diagonal') {
353	    const size = 8;
354	    canvas.width = size;
355	    canvas.height = size;
356	    ctx.strokeStyle = color;
357	    ctx.lineWidth = 1;
358	    ctx.beginPath();
359	    ctx.moveTo(size, 0);
360	    ctx.lineTo(0, size);
361	    ctx.stroke();
362	    // Wrap the line so the tile repeats seamlessly
363	    ctx.beginPath();
364	    ctx.moveTo(size + size, 0);
365	    ctx.lineTo(0, size + size);
366	    ctx.stroke();
367	  } else {
368	    canvas.width = direction === 'vertical' ? 9 : 1;
369	    canvas.height = direction === 'vertical' ? 1 : 9;
370	    ctx.fillStyle = color;
371	    if (direction === 'vertical') {
372	      ctx.fillRect(8, 0, 1, 1);
373	    } else {
374	      ctx.fillRect(0, 8, 1, 1);
375	    }
376	  }
377	
378	  return canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
379	}
380	
381	/** Export a single slide as a PNG download */
382	async function exportSlide(page, type) {
383	  const slideEl = document.getElementById(`slide-${page}`);
384	  if (!slideEl) return;
385	
386	  const btn = slideEl.closest('.slide-wrapper').querySelector('.slide-export-btn');
387	  btn.textContent = 'Rendering…';
388	  btn.disabled = true;
389	
390	  try {
391	    const { default: html2canvas } = await import('html2canvas');
392	    const canvas = await html2canvas(slideEl, {
393	      scale: 2,
394	      useCORS: true,
395	      logging: false,
396	      backgroundColor: null,
397	    });
398	
399	    const link = document.createElement('a');
400	    link.download = `slide-${String(page).padStart(2, '0')}-${type}.png`;
401	    link.href = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
402	    link.click();
403	  } catch (e) {
404	    console.error('Export failed:', e);
405	  }
406	
407	  btn.textContent = 'Export PNG';
408	  btn.disabled = false;
409	}
410	
411	/** Export all slides as PNGs saved to the export/ folder on disk */
412	async function exportAllSlides() {
413	  const btn = document.getElementById('btn-export-all');
414	  if (!btn) return;
415	  btn.disabled = true;
416	
417	  const totalSlides = currentContent.slides.length;
418	  const savedPage = currentPage;
419	  const totalPages = Math.ceil(totalSlides / SLIDES_PER_PAGE);
420	  const { default: html2canvas } = await import('html2canvas');
421	  const pendingSaves = [];
422	
423	  /** Capture all visible slides and fire save requests */
424	  async function capturePage(pageSlides, startIdx, subdir) {
425	    const capturePromises = pageSlides.map((slide, j) => {
426	      const i = startIdx + j;
427	      const page = i + 1;
428	      const slideEl = document.getElementById(`slide-${page}`);
429	      if (!slideEl) return Promise.resolve();
430	
431	      const slideWidth = slideEl.offsetWidth;
432	      const exportScale = Math.ceil(3840 / slideWidth);
433	      return html2canvas(slideEl, {
434	        scale: exportScale,
435	        useCORS: true,
436	        logging: false,
437	        backgroundColor: null,
438	      }).then(canvas => ({
439	        filename: `slide-${String(page).padStart(2, '0')}-${slide.type}.png`,
440	        dataUrl: canvas.toDataURL('image/png'),
441	      })).catch(e => {
442	        console.error(`Capture slide ${page} failed:`, e);
443	        return null;
444	      });
445	    });
446	
447	    const captured = (await Promise.all(capturePromises)).filter(Boolean);
448	
449	    const savePromises = captured.map(({ filename, dataUrl }) =>
450	      fetch('/api/save-slide', {
451	        method: 'POST',
452	        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
453	        body: JSON.stringify({ filename, dataUrl, subdir }),
454	      }).catch(e => console.error(`Save ${filename} failed:`, e))
455	    );
456	
457	    pendingSaves.push(...savePromises);
458	  }
459	
460	  for (let pg = 0; pg < totalPages; pg++) {
461	    setPage(pg);
462	    renderDeck();
463	    fixStatBlockContrast();
464	    fitStatSizes();
465	
466	    // Wait for all WebGL shapes to finish rendering
467	    await deckPostInitsReady;
468	
469	    const startIdx = pg * SLIDES_PER_PAGE;
470	    const pageSlides = currentContent.slides.slice(startIdx, startIdx + SLIDES_PER_PAGE);
471	
472	    // ── 16:9 capture ──
473	    btn.textContent = `16:9 page ${pg + 1}/${totalPages}…`;
474	    await capturePage(pageSlides, startIdx, '16x9');
475	
476	    // ── 4:3 capture — full re-render at 4:3 so droplets get fresh content textures ──
477	    btn.textContent = `4:3 page ${pg + 1}/${totalPages}…`;
478	    document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--export-aspect', '4 / 3');
479	    renderDeck();
480	    fixStatBlockContrast();
481	    fitStatSizes();
482	    await deckPostInitsReady;
483	    await capturePage(pageSlides, startIdx, '4x3');
484	    document.documentElement.style.removeProperty('--export-aspect');
485	  }
486	
487	  // Wait for all saves to finish before building the deck
488	  btn.textContent = `Optimizing PNGs…`;
489	  await Promise.all(pendingSaves);
490	
491	  // Build PPTX and PDF from the exported PNGs
492	  btn.textContent = 'Building PPTX + PDF…';
493	  try {
494	    const buildRes = await fetch('/api/build-deck', { method: 'POST' });
495	    const buildData = await buildRes.json();
496	    if (buildData.ok) {
497	      btn.textContent = `Done — ${buildData.slides} slides`;
498	    } else {
499	      btn.textContent = 'PNG done, deck failed';
500	      console.error('Build deck failed:', buildData.error);
501	    }
502	  } catch (e) {
503	    console.error('Build deck failed:', e);
504	    btn.textContent = 'PNG done, deck failed';
505	  }
506	
507	  // Restore original page
508	  setPage(savedPage);
509	  renderDeck();
510	  setTimeout(() => { btn.textContent = 'Export All'; btn.disabled = false; }, 3000);
511	}

606	        input.addEventListener('input', () => {

608	          markDirty();

610	        });

613	        group.appendChild(input);
614	        panel.appendChild(group);

619	  if (slide.type === 'data' && slide.stats) {

630	        const label = document.createElement('label');

632	        label.textContent = key;
633

635	        input.className = 'edit-input';
636	        input.value = stat[key] ?? '';
637

652	}
653	
654	/** Re-render a single slide in place (without tearing down the whole deck) */
655	function renderSingleSlide(slideIndex) {
656	  const config = currentConfig;
657	  const slide = currentContent.slides[slideIndex];
658	  const page = slideIndex + 1;
659	
660	  const slideEl = document.getElementById(`slide-${page}`);
661	  if (!slideEl) return;
662	
663	  const layoutName = config.layouts[slide.type] || Object.values(config.layouts)[0];
664	  const layoutFn = layoutRegistry[layoutName];
665	  if (!layoutFn) return;
666	
667	  slideEl.innerHTML = layoutFn(slide, page);
668	
669	  const inner = slideEl.querySelector('.slide-inner');
670	  if (!inner) return;
671	
672	  // Re-inject textures (shapes are NOT re-injected to avoid WebGL churn)
673	  if (config.pinstripes !== 'none') {
674	    inner.insertAdjacentHTML('afterbegin', '<div class="shape-overlay texture-pinstripes"></div>');
675	  }
676	  inner.insertAdjacentHTML(
677	    'afterbegin',
678	    `<div class="shape-overlay texture-noise" style="background-image:url(${noiseDataURL});opacity:${config.noise / 100}"></div>`,
679	  );
680	
681	  // Fix stat block contrast if this is a data slide
682	  slideEl.querySelectorAll('.stat-block').forEach(block => {
683	    const blockL = luminance(getComputedStyle(block).backgroundColor);
684	    block.style.color = blockL > 0.18 ? '#1B2A3D' : '#FFFFFF';
685	    const inner2 = block.closest('.slide-inner');
686	    if (inner2) {
687	      const slideL = luminance(getComputedStyle(inner2).backgroundColor);
688	      block.style.border = Math.abs(blockL - slideL) < 0.05 ? '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.15)' : '';
689	    }
690	  });
691	
692	  // Fit stat value sizes for this slide — all match the longest text
693	  const statValues = slideEl.querySelectorAll('.stat-value');
694	  if (statValues.length > 0) {
695	    statValues.forEach(v => { v.style.fontSize = ''; v.style.whiteSpace = 'nowrap'; });
696	    let minFitted = Infinity;
697	    statValues.forEach(v => {
698	      const block = v.closest('.stat-block');
699	      const padL = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(block).paddingLeft);
700	      const padR = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(block).paddingRight);
701	      const available = block.clientWidth - padL - padR;
702	      const currentSize = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(v).fontSize);
703	      const textWidth = v.scrollWidth;
704	      if (textWidth > 0) minFitted = Math.min(minFitted, currentSize * (available / textWidth));
705	    });
706	    if (minFitted !== Infinity) {
707	      const firstBlock = statValues[0].closest('.stat-block');
708	      const blockH = firstBlock.clientHeight - parseFloat(getComputedStyle(firstBlock).paddingTop) - parseFloat(getComputedStyle(firstBlock).paddingBottom);
709	      const finalSize = Math.min(minFitted, blockH * 0.55);
710	      statValues.forEach(v => v.style.fontSize = `${finalSize}px`);
711	    }
712	  }
713	}
714	
715	/** Relative luminance from an rgb() or rgba() string */
716	function luminance(cssColor) {
717	  const match = cssColor.match(/(\d+),\s*(\d+),\s*(\d+)/);
718	  if (!match) return 0;
719	  const [r, g, b] = [match[1], match[2], match[3]].map(c => {
720	    const s = Number(c) / 255;
721	    return s <= 0.03928 ? s / 12.92 : Math.pow((s + 0.055) / 1.055, 2.4);
722	  });
723	  return 0.2126 * r + 0.7152 * g + 0.0722 * b;
724	}
725	
726	/** Scale stat values so the longest text fills its block width, and all others match that size */
727	function fitStatSizes() {
728	  document.querySelectorAll('.data-big-numbers').forEach(slide => {
729	    const values = slide.querySelectorAll('.stat-value');
730	    if (values.length === 0) return;
731	
732	    // Reset and prevent wrapping for measurement
733	    values.forEach(v => { v.style.fontSize = ''; v.style.whiteSpace = 'nowrap'; });
734	
735	    // Find the size that makes the longest text exactly fill its block
736	    let minFitted = Infinity;
737	    values.forEach(v => {
738	      const block = v.closest('.stat-block');
739	      const padL = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(block).paddingLeft);
740	      const padR = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(block).paddingRight);
741	      const available = block.clientWidth - padL - padR;
742	      const currentSize = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(v).fontSize);
743	      const textWidth = v.scrollWidth;
744	      if (textWidth > 0) {
745	        minFitted = Math.min(minFitted, currentSize * (available / textWidth));
746	      }
747	    });
748	
749	    if (minFitted !== Infinity) {
750	      // Cap at 55% of block height so label text still fits
751	      const firstBlock = values[0].closest('.stat-block');
752	      const blockH = firstBlock.clientHeight - parseFloat(getComputedStyle(firstBlock).paddingTop) - parseFloat(getComputedStyle(firstBlock).paddingBottom);
753	      const finalSize = Math.min(minFitted, blockH * 0.55);
754	      values.forEach(v => v.style.fontSize = `${finalSize}px`);
755	    }
756	  });
757	}
758	
759	/** Set adaptive text colour on stat blocks and add border when block disappears into slide bg */
760	function fixStatBlockContrast() {
761	  document.querySelectorAll('.stat-block').forEach(block => {
762	    const blockL = luminance(getComputedStyle(block).backgroundColor);
763	    block.style.color = blockL > 0.18 ? '#1B2A3D' : '#FFFFFF';
764	
765	    // Add border when block blends into slide background
766	    const slide = block.closest('.slide-inner');
767	    if (slide) {
768	      const slideL = luminance(getComputedStyle(slide).backgroundColor);
769	      const contrast = Math.abs(blockL - slideL);
770	      block.style.border = contrast < 0.05 ? '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.15)' : '';
771	    }
772	  });
773	}
774	
775	/** Render all slides into the #deck container */
776	function renderDeck() {
777	  const config = currentConfig;
778	  const content = currentContent;
779	  const deck = document.getElementById('deck');
780	
781	  // Clean up previous WebGL contexts before clearing DOM
782	  cleanupShapes();
783	  deck.innerHTML = '';
784	
785	  // Collect any post-DOM init callbacks (for WebGL shapes)
786	  const postInits = [];

788	  const startIdx = currentPage * SLIDES_PER_PAGE;

790	
791	  pageSlides.forEach((slide, j) => {
792	    const i = startIdx + j;
793	    const page = i + 1;
794	
795	    // Determine layout for this slide type
796	    const layoutName = config.layouts[slide.type] || Object.values(config.layouts)[0];
797	    const layoutFn = layoutRegistry[layoutName];
798	
799	    if (!layoutFn) {
800	      console.warn(`Layout "${layoutName}" not found for slide type "${slide.type}"`);
801	      return;
802	    }
803	
804	    // Create wrapper
805	    const wrapper = document.createElement('div');
806	    wrapper.className = 'slide-wrapper';
807	
808	    // Label + per-slide shape control + export button
809	    const labelRow = document.createElement('div');
810	    labelRow.className = 'slide-label';
811	
812	    const labelText = document.createElement('span');
813	    labelText.textContent = `${String(page).padStart(2, '0')} — ${slide.type} — ${layoutName}`;
814	
815	    const slideShape = config.slideShapes[i] ?? config.shapes;
816	    const shapeSelect = document.createElement('select');
817	    shapeSelect.className = 'ctrl-select slide-shape-select';
818	    SHAPES.forEach(opt => {
819	      const o = document.createElement('option');
820	      o.value = opt.value;
821	      o.textContent = opt.label;
822	      if (opt.value === slideShape) o.selected = true;
823	      shapeSelect.appendChild(o);
824	    });
825	    shapeSelect.addEventListener('change', e => {
826	      currentConfig.slideShapes[i] = e.target.value;
827	      markDirty();
828	      renderDeck();
829	    });
830	
831	    const editBtn = document.createElement('button');

217	      <div class="statement-label">${slide.section || ''}</div>
218	      <div class="statement-text">${text}</div>
219	      ${slide.attribution ? `<div class="statement-attribution">${slide.attribution}</div>` : ''}
220	    </div>
221	  `;
222	}
223	
224	// ─── HELPERS ────────────────────────────────────
225	
226	function escapeRegex(str) {
227	  if (!str) return '';
228	  return str.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
229	}
230	
231	// ─── LAYOUT REGISTRY ────────────────────────────
232	
233	export const layoutRegistry = {
234	  // Covers
235	  'cover-bold-full':      coverBoldFull,
236	  'cover-dark-minimal':   coverDarkMinimal,
237	
238	  // Dividers
239	  'divider-color-burst':     dividerColorBurst,
240	  'divider-dark-cinematic':  dividerDarkCinematic,

249	
250	  // Statement

253	};

1	/**
2	 * Shape generators — return HTML strings or { html, init } objects for overlays.
3	 *
4	 * Simple shapes return an HTML string.
5	 * WebGL shapes (droplets) return { html, init } where init(slideEl) sets up the
6	 * Three.js scene after the element is in the DOM.
7	 */
8	import { initDropletCanvas, initDropletCanvasTinted, initDropletCanvasOil, initDropletCanvasMercury, initDropletCanvasFrosted, initDropletCanvasIce, disposeDroplets } from './shapes-droplets.js';
9	
10	/** Call before re-rendering the deck to clean up WebGL contexts */
11	export function cleanupShapes() {
12	  disposeDroplets();
13	}
14	
15	/** No shapes */
16	export function shapesNone() {
17	  return '';
18	}
19	
20	/**
21	 * Pills / capsules — inspired by WeTransfer
22	 * Scattered rounded rectangles at various angles and sizes
23	 */
24	export function shapesPills(colors) {
25	  const c = colors || ['var(--color-shape-1)', 'var(--color-shape-2)', 'var(--color-shape-3)'];
26	  return `
27	    <div class="shape-overlay" style="
28	      top: -4%; right: 8%;
29	      width: 9rem; height: 3.2rem;
30	      background: ${c[0]};
31	      border-radius: 100px;
32	      transform: rotate(-15deg);
33	      opacity: 0.7;
34	    "></div>
35	    <div class="shape-overlay" style="
36	      bottom: 12%; right: -2%;
37	      width: 7rem; height: 2.6rem;
38	      background: ${c[1]};
39	      border-radius: 100px;
40	      transform: rotate(25deg);
41	      opacity: 0.6;
42	    "></div>
43	    <div class="shape-overlay" style="
44	      top: 30%; right: 3%;
45	      width: 2.2rem; height: 2.2rem;
46	      background: ${c[2]};
47	      border-radius: 50%;
48	      opacity: 0.5;
49	    "></div>
50	    <div class="shape-overlay" style="
51	      bottom: 5%; left: 15%;
52	      width: 6rem; height: 2.2rem;
53	      background: ${c[0]};
54	      border-radius: 100px;
55	      transform: rotate(40deg);
56	      opacity: 0.35;
57	    "></div>
58	  `;
59	}
60	
61	/**
62	 * Organic blobs — inspired by Ace & Tate
63	 * Amorphous, soft-edged shapes bleeding off edges
64	 */
65	export function shapesBlobs(colors) {
66	  const c = colors || ['var(--color-shape-1)', 'var(--color-shape-2)', 'var(--color-shape-3)'];
67	  return `
68	    <svg class="shape-overlay" style="top: -15%; right: -10%; width: 45%; height: 60%; opacity: 0.25;"
69	         viewBox="0 0 200 200" preserveAspectRatio="none">
70	      <path d="M 45,-10 C 100,-20 170,30 180,90 C 190,150 140,200 80,190 C 20,180 -10,120 10,70 C 25,30 20,5 45,-10 Z"
71	            fill="${c[0]}" />
72	    </svg>
73	    <svg class="shape-overlay" style="bottom: -20%; left: -8%; width: 35%; height: 50%; opacity: 0.2;"
74	         viewBox="0 0 200 200" preserveAspectRatio="none">
75	      <path d="M 30,20 C 80,-10 160,20 170,80 C 180,140 130,190 70,180 C 10,170 -20,110 10,60 C 25,35 10,30 30,20 Z"
76	            fill="${c[1]}" />
77	    </svg>
78	  `;
79	}
80	
81	/**
82	 * Colour blocks — inspired by Artsy
83	 * Rectangular blocks with white gaps
84	 */
85	export function shapesBlocks(colors) {
86	  const c = colors || ['var(--color-shape-1)', 'var(--color-shape-2)', 'var(--color-shape-3)'];
87	  return `

89	      top: 0; right: 0;
90	      width: 18%; height: 35%;
91	      background: ${c[0]};

96	      width: 12%; height: 20%;

103	      background: ${c[2]};
104	      opacity: 0.9;

107	}
108	
109	/**

112	 */
113	export function shapesGoldenRect(colors) {
114	  const c = colors || ['var(--color-shape-1)'];
115	  return `
116	    <svg class="shape-overlay" style="top: 5%; right: 5%; width: 40%; height: 80%; opacity: 0.12;"
117	         viewBox="0 0 200 320" fill="none" stroke="${c[0]}" stroke-width="1">
118	      <rect x="10" y="10" width="180" height="290" rx="0" />

122	      <circle cx="100" cy="100" r="55" />
123	    </svg>
124	  `;

127	/**
128	 * Water droplets — WebGL metaballs with refraction + specular
129	 * Returns { html, init } — canvas is inserted as HTML, Three.js starts in init().
130	 */
131	export function shapesDroplets(slideIndex) {

134	    init: (slideEl) => initDropletCanvas(slideEl, slideIndex),

 `./src/shapes-droplets.js`

```text
1	/**
2	 * WebGL water droplets — metaballs with real content refraction.
3	 *
4	 * Captures the slide's rendered HTML as a texture via html2canvas,
5	 * then refracts it through a metaball field with chromatic aberration,
6	 * Fresnel rim, and dual specular highlights.
7	 *
8	 * The canvas sits ON TOP of all content (z-index: 10) so the droplets
9	 * visually distort the text and cards beneath them.
10	 */
11	import * as THREE from 'three';
12	import { RGBELoader } from 'three/addons/loaders/RGBELoader.js';
13	import html2canvas from 'html2canvas';
14	
15	const MAX_DROPLETS = 96;
16	const ENV_HDR_PATH = '/env-theater.hdr';
17	
18	// Shared env texture — loaded once, reused across all slides
19	let envTexturePromise = null;
20	
21	function loadEnvTexture() {
22	  if (!envTexturePromise) {
23	    envTexturePromise = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
24	      new RGBELoader().load(
25	        ENV_HDR_PATH,
26	        (texture) => {
27	          texture.mapping = THREE.EquirectangularReflectionMapping;
28	          texture.wrapS = THREE.RepeatWrapping;
29	          texture.wrapT = THREE.ClampToEdgeWrapping;
30	          texture.minFilter = THREE.LinearFilter;
31	          texture.magFilter = THREE.LinearFilter;
32	          resolve(texture);
33	        },
34	        undefined,
35	        reject,
36	      );
37	    });
38	  }
39	  return envTexturePromise;
40	}
41	
42	// ─── SHADERS ────────────────────────────────────
43	
44	const vertexShader = /* glsl */ `
45	  varying vec2 vUv;
46	  void main() {
47	    vUv = uv;
48	    gl_Position = projectionMatrix * modelViewMatrix * vec4(position, 1.0);
49	  }
50	`;
51	
52	const fragmentShader = /* glsl */ `
53	  precision highp float;
54	  varying vec2 vUv;
55	
56	  uniform vec2 uResolution;
57	  uniform sampler2D uContentTex;              // captured slide content
58	  uniform sampler2D uEnvMap;                  // equirectangular environment map
59	  uniform vec3 uDroplets[${MAX_DROPLETS}];    // .xy = centre (UV), .z = radius
60	  uniform vec2 uLightPos;                     // light position (UV space)
61	
62	  const float THRESHOLD = 0.45;
63	  const float R0 = 0.02;   // Schlick's R₀ for water: ((1.33-1)/(1.33+1))²
64	
65	  // ── Metaball field (Gaussian kernel) ──
66	  float field(vec2 p, float aspect) {
67	    float sum = 0.0;
68	    for (int i = 0; i < ${MAX_DROPLETS}; i++) {
69	      vec2 centre = uDroplets[i].xy;
70	      float radius = uDroplets[i].z;
71	      if (radius < 0.001) continue;
72	      vec2 diff = p - centre;
73	      diff.x *= aspect;
74	      sum += exp(-dot(diff, diff) / (2.0 * radius * radius));
75	    }
76	    return sum;
77	  }
78	
79	  // ── Surface normal ──
80	  // Real meniscus: exponential decay from the contact line inward,
81	  // matching h(x) = h₀(1 - e^(-x/λ_c)). The interior is dead flat;
82	  // curvature lives only in a narrow rim at the edge.
83	  vec3 getNormal(vec2 p, float f, float aspect) {
84	    float e = 0.004;
85	    float l = field(p - vec2(e, 0.0), aspect);
86	    float r = field(p + vec2(e, 0.0), aspect);
87	    float d = field(p - vec2(0.0, e), aspect);
88	    float u = field(p + vec2(0.0, e), aspect);
89	
90	    // Exponential meniscus profile: steep at contact line, rapid decay inward
91	    float distFromEdge = max(f - THRESHOLD, 0.0);
92	    float meniscus = exp(-distFromEdge * 8.0);
93	
94	    return normalize(vec3((l - r) * meniscus, (d - u) * meniscus, e * 55.0));
95	  }
96	
97	  // ── Equirectangular environment map lookup ──
98	  vec3 envMap(vec3 dir) {
99	    float phi   = atan(dir.z, dir.x);
100	    float theta = asin(clamp(dir.y, -1.0, 1.0));
101	    vec2 envUV  = vec2(phi / 6.28318 + 0.5, theta / 3.14159 + 0.5);
102	    vec3 hdr    = texture2D(uEnvMap, envUV).rgb;
103	    // Reinhard tonemap with slight exposure boost to keep bright sources visible
104	    hdr *= 1.5;
105	    return hdr / (hdr + 1.0);
106	  }
107	
108	  void main() {
109	    vec2 uv = vUv;
110	    float aspect = uResolution.x / uResolution.y;
111	    float f = field(uv, aspect);
112	
113	    // ── Outside: caustic light band ──
114	    // The meniscus focuses transmitted light just outside the contact line,
115	    // creating a thin bright caustic on the surface.
116	    if (f < THRESHOLD) {
117	      float caustic = smoothstep(THRESHOLD * 0.65, THRESHOLD * 0.92, f)
118	                    * (1.0 - smoothstep(THRESHOLD * 0.92, THRESHOLD, f));
119	      gl_FragColor = vec4(vec3(1.0), caustic * 0.07);
120	      return;
121	    }
122	
123	    // ── Inside the water ──
124	    vec3 N = getNormal(uv, f, aspect);
125	
126	    // Meniscus factor: 1 at contact line, 0 in flat interior
127	    float distFromEdge = max(f - THRESHOLD, 0.0);
128	    float meniscus = exp(-distFromEdge * 8.0);
129	
130	    // ── Refraction ──
131	    // Flat interior: water is ~5mm thick, negligible offset at normal incidence.
132	    // Meniscus: curved surface bends light — refraction concentrates here.
133	    float refractionStrength = 0.012 * meniscus;
134	    vec2 refractedUV = clamp(uv + N.xy * refractionStrength, 0.0, 1.0);
135	
136	    // ── Chromatic dispersion ──
137	    // Δn ≈ 0.006 between red and blue in water.
138	    // Only visible where there's curvature (the meniscus).
139	    float chromatic = 0.015 * meniscus;
140	    vec3 contentColor;
141	    contentColor.r = texture2D(uContentTex, clamp(refractedUV + N.xy * chromatic, 0.0, 1.0)).r;
142	    contentColor.g = texture2D(uContentTex, refractedUV).g;
143	    contentColor.b = texture2D(uContentTex, clamp(refractedUV - N.xy * chromatic, 0.0, 1.0)).b;
144	
145	    // ── Caustic brightening inside the meniscus ──
146	    // The curved meniscus acts as a lens, focusing light into a bright
147	    // band just inward of the contact line.
148	    float causticInner = meniscus * (1.0 - meniscus) * 4.0;
149	    contentColor *= 1.0 + causticInner * 0.15;
150	
151	    // ── Perspective view direction ──
152	    vec3 V = normalize(vec3((uv - 0.5) * vec2(aspect, 1.0) * 0.7, -1.0));
153	
154	    // ── Fresnel (Schlick's approximation for water n=1.33) ──
155	    float NdV = max(dot(N, -V), 0.0);
156	    float fresnel = R0 + (1.0 - R0) * pow(1.0 - NdV, 5.0);
157	
158	    // ── Environment reflection ──
159	    vec3 R = reflect(V, N);
160	    vec3 envColor = envMap(R);
161	
162	    // ── Specular ──
163	    vec3 L = normalize(vec3((uLightPos - uv) * vec2(aspect, 1.0), 0.85));
164	    vec3 H = normalize(L - V);
165	    float NdH = max(dot(N, H), 0.0);
166	    // Flat interior: mirror-tight specular (very high power)
167	    float specMirror = pow(NdH, 512.0);
168	    // Meniscus: broader spread from curved surface
169	    float specBroad = pow(NdH, 40.0) * meniscus;
170	
171	    // ── Composite ──
172	    vec3 color = contentColor;
173	
174	    // Fresnel reflection — physically: 2% on flat interior, up to 100% at rim
175	    color = mix(color, envColor, fresnel);
176	
177	    // Specular highlights — subdued point light
178	    color += specMirror * 0.25;
179	    color += specBroad * 0.03;
180	
181	    // ── Alpha: water film tapers to zero thickness at the contact line ──
182	    float alpha = smoothstep(THRESHOLD, THRESHOLD + 0.045, f);
183	
184	    gl_FragColor = vec4(color, alpha);
185	  }
186	`;
187	
188	// ─── SEEDED PRNG (Mulberry32) ───────────────────
189	
190	function mulberry32(seed) {
191	  return function () {
192	    seed |= 0;
193	    seed = (seed + 0x6d2b79f5) | 0;
194	    let t = Math.imul(seed ^ (seed >>> 15), 1 | seed);
195	    t = (t + Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 7), 61 | t)) ^ t;
196	    return ((t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296;
197	  };
198	}
199	
200	// ─── GENERATE DROPLET POSITIONS ─────────────────
201	
202	function generateDroplets(seed) {
203	  const rng = mulberry32(seed * 13397 + 7);
204	  const drops = [];
205	
206	  const clamp01 = (v) => Math.max(0.03, Math.min(0.97, v));
207	
208	  // 3–5 puddle groups
209	  const puddleCount = 3 + Math.floor(rng() * 3);
210	
211	  for (let p = 0; p < puddleCount; p++) {
212	    if (drops.length >= MAX_DROPLETS) break;
213	
214	    // Core: 3–4 larger blobs forming a chunky central body
215	    const cx = 0.15 + rng() * 0.7;
216	    const cy = 0.15 + rng() * 0.7;
217	    const coreCount = 3 + Math.floor(rng() * 2);
218	    for (let c = 0; c < coreCount; c++) {
219	      if (drops.length >= MAX_DROPLETS) break;
220	      const a = rng() * Math.PI * 2;
221	      const d = rng() * 0.045;
222	      drops.push(new THREE.Vector3(
223	        clamp01(cx + Math.cos(a) * d),
224	        clamp01(cy + Math.sin(a) * d),
225	        0.04 + rng() * 0.03,
226	      ));
227	    }
228	
229	    // Arms / peninsulas: 3–5 short chains radiating outward
230	    const armCount = 3 + Math.floor(rng() * 3);
231	    for (let arm = 0; arm < armCount; arm++) {
232	      if (drops.length >= MAX_DROPLETS) break;
233	      const armAngle = rng() * Math.PI * 2;
234	      const armLen = 2 + Math.floor(rng() * 2); // 2–3 drops per arm
235	      let ax = cx, ay = cy;
236	      let armR = 0.03 + rng() * 0.02;
237	
238	      for (let j = 0; j < armLen; j++) {
239	        if (drops.length >= MAX_DROPLETS) break;
240	        const stepDist = armR * (1.0 + rng() * 0.6);
241	        const wander = (rng() - 0.5) * 0.5;
242	        ax += Math.cos(armAngle + wander) * stepDist;
243	        ay += Math.sin(armAngle + wander) * stepDist;
244	        armR *= 0.75 + rng() * 0.15;
245	        drops.push(new THREE.Vector3(
246	          clamp01(ax),
247	          clamp01(ay),
248	          Math.max(0.012, armR),
249	        ));
250	      }
251	    }
252	
253	    // Scattered satellites: a couple of small drops nearby
254	    const satCount = 1 + Math.floor(rng() * 2);
255	    for (let s = 0; s < satCount; s++) {
256	      if (drops.length >= MAX_DROPLETS) break;
257	      const a = rng() * Math.PI * 2;
258	      const d = 0.06 + rng() * 0.06;
259	      drops.push(new THREE.Vector3(
260	        clamp01(cx + Math.cos(a) * d),
261	        clamp01(cy + Math.sin(a) * d),
262	        0.008 + rng() * 0.012,
263	      ));
264	    }
265	  }
266	
267	  // Lone scattered drops across the whole slide
268	  const scatterCount = 15 + Math.floor(rng() * 10); // 15–24
269	  for (let s = 0; s < scatterCount; s++) {
270	    if (drops.length >= MAX_DROPLETS) break;
271	    drops.push(new THREE.Vector3(
272	      0.04 + rng() * 0.92,
273	      0.04 + rng() * 0.92,
274	      0.004 + rng() * 0.01,
275	    ));
276	  }
277	
278	  // Pad with zero-radius (invisible)
279	  while (drops.length < MAX_DROPLETS) {
280	    drops.push(new THREE.Vector3(0, 0, 0));
281	  }
282	  return drops;
283	}
284	
285	// ─── TINTED FRAGMENT SHADER ─────────────────────
286	// Same optics but with Beer-Lambert absorption through coloured water.
287	// uWaterColor controls which wavelengths pass through; depth is derived
288	// from the metaball field so the tint is strongest at puddle centres.
289	
290	const fragmentShaderTinted = /* glsl */ `
291	  precision highp float;
292	  varying vec2 vUv;
293	
294	  uniform vec2 uResolution;
295	  uniform sampler2D uContentTex;
296	  uniform sampler2D uEnvMap;
297	  uniform vec3 uDroplets[${MAX_DROPLETS}];
298	  uniform vec2 uLightPos;
299	  uniform vec3 uWaterColor;
300	
301	  const float THRESHOLD = 0.45;
302	  const float R0 = 0.02;
303	
304	  float field(vec2 p, float aspect) {
305	    float sum = 0.0;
306	    for (int i = 0; i < ${MAX_DROPLETS}; i++) {
307	      vec2 centre = uDroplets[i].xy;
308	      float radius = uDroplets[i].z;
309	      if (radius < 0.001) continue;
310	      vec2 diff = p - centre;
311	      diff.x *= aspect;
312	      sum += exp(-dot(diff, diff) / (2.0 * radius * radius));
313	    }
314	    return sum;
315	  }
316	
317	  vec3 getNormal(vec2 p, float f, float aspect) {
318	    float e = 0.004;
319	    float l = field(p - vec2(e, 0.0), aspect);
320	    float r = field(p + vec2(e, 0.0), aspect);
321	    float d = field(p - vec2(0.0, e), aspect);
322	    float u = field(p + vec2(0.0, e), aspect);
323	    float distFromEdge = max(f - THRESHOLD, 0.0);
324	    float meniscus = exp(-distFromEdge * 8.0);
325	    return normalize(vec3((l - r) * meniscus, (d - u) * meniscus, e * 55.0));
326	  }
327	
328	  vec3 envMap(vec3 dir) {
329	    float phi   = atan(dir.z, dir.x);
330	    float theta = asin(clamp(dir.y, -1.0, 1.0));
331	    vec2 envUV  = vec2(phi / 6.28318 + 0.5, theta / 3.14159 + 0.5);
332	    vec3 hdr    = texture2D(uEnvMap, envUV).rgb;
333	    hdr *= 1.5;
334	    return hdr / (hdr + 1.0);
335	  }
336	
337	  void main() {
338	    vec2 uv = vUv;
339	    float aspect = uResolution.x / uResolution.y;
340	    float f = field(uv, aspect);
341	
342	    // Outside: caustic band picks up a faint water colour
343	    if (f < THRESHOLD) {
344	      float caustic = smoothstep(THRESHOLD * 0.65, THRESHOLD * 0.92, f)
345	                    * (1.0 - smoothstep(THRESHOLD * 0.92, THRESHOLD, f));
346	      vec3 causticColor = mix(vec3(1.0), uWaterColor, 0.3);
347	      gl_FragColor = vec4(causticColor, caustic * 0.09);
348	      return;
349	    }
350	
351	    vec3 N = getNormal(uv, f, aspect);
352	    float distFromEdge = max(f - THRESHOLD, 0.0);
353	    float meniscus = exp(-distFromEdge * 8.0);
354	
355	    // Refraction
356	    float refractionStrength = 0.012 * meniscus;
357	    vec2 refractedUV = clamp(uv + N.xy * refractionStrength, 0.0, 1.0);
358	
359	    // Chromatic dispersion
360	    float chromatic = 0.015 * meniscus;
361	    vec3 contentColor;
362	    contentColor.r = texture2D(uContentTex, clamp(refractedUV + N.xy * chromatic, 0.0, 1.0)).r;
363	    contentColor.g = texture2D(uContentTex, refractedUV).g;
364	    contentColor.b = texture2D(uContentTex, clamp(refractedUV - N.xy * chromatic, 0.0, 1.0)).b;
365	
366	    // Caustic brightening
367	    float causticInner = meniscus * (1.0 - meniscus) * 4.0;
368	    contentColor *= 1.0 + causticInner * 0.15;
369	
370	    // Beer-Lambert absorption through tinted water
371	    // Depth increases toward the centre of each puddle
372	    float waterDepth = smoothstep(THRESHOLD, THRESHOLD + 0.2, f);
373	    // Wavelengths complementary to uWaterColor are absorbed exponentially
374	    vec3 absorption = exp(-((1.0 - uWaterColor) * waterDepth * 2.5));
375	    contentColor *= absorption;
376	    // Subtle forward-scattering adds the water's own hue
377	    contentColor += uWaterColor * waterDepth * 0.06;
378	
379	    // Perspective view direction
380	    vec3 V = normalize(vec3((uv - 0.5) * vec2(aspect, 1.0) * 0.7, -1.0));
381	
382	    // Fresnel
383	    float NdV = max(dot(N, -V), 0.0);
384	    float fresnel = R0 + (1.0 - R0) * pow(1.0 - NdV, 5.0);
385	
386	    // Environment reflection — lightly tinted by the water
387	    vec3 R = reflect(V, N);
388	    vec3 envColor = envMap(R);
389	    envColor = mix(envColor, envColor * uWaterColor, 0.2);
390	
391	    // Specular
392	    vec3 L = normalize(vec3((uLightPos - uv) * vec2(aspect, 1.0), 0.85));
393	    vec3 H = normalize(L - V);
394	    float NdH = max(dot(N, H), 0.0);
395	    float specMirror = pow(NdH, 512.0);
396	    float specBroad = pow(NdH, 40.0) * meniscus;
397	
398	    // Composite
399	    vec3 color = contentColor;
400	    color = mix(color, envColor, fresnel);
```

409	// ─── CRUDE OIL FRAGMENT SHADER ─────────────────
410	// Opaque dark fluid with thin-film iridescence at the meniscus,
411	// higher refractive index (n ≈ 1.5), and heavy Beer-Lambert absorption.

414	  precision highp float;

431	      vec2 centre = uDroplets[i].xy;
432	      float radius = uDroplets[i].z;

439	  }
440	
441	  vec3 getNormal(vec2 p, float f, float aspect) {
442	    float e = 0.004;
443	    float l = field(p - vec2(e, 0.0), aspect);
444	    float r = field(p + vec2(e, 0.0), aspect);
445	    float d = field(p - vec2(0.0, e), aspect);
446	    float u = field(p + vec2(0.0, e), aspect);
447	    float distFromEdge = max(f - THRESHOLD, 0.0);
448	    float meniscus = exp(-distFromEdge * 8.0);
449	    return normalize(vec3((l - r) * meniscus, (d - u) * meniscus, e * 55.0));
450	  }
451	
452	  vec3 envMap(vec3 dir) {
453	    float phi   = atan(dir.z, dir.x);
454	    float theta = asin(clamp(dir.y, -1.0, 1.0));
455	    vec2 envUV  = vec2(phi / 6.28318 + 0.5, theta / 3.14159 + 0.5);

457	    hdr *= 1.5;
458	    return hdr / (hdr + 1.0);
459	  }
460	
461	  // Thin-film interference — oil slick rainbow at the meniscus
462	  // Film thickness varies with depth; visible colour cycles through
463	  // the spectrum as optical path difference changes.
464	  vec3 thinFilm(float thickness) {
465	    // Approximate spectral interference for a ~300-600nm oil film
466	    // by cycling RGB at different rates

468	    return vec3(
469	      0.5 + 0.5 * cos(6.28318 * (d + 0.0)),
470	      0.5 + 0.5 * cos(6.28318 * (d + 0.33)),
471	      0.5 + 0.5 * cos(6.28318 * (d + 0.67))
472	    );
473	  }
474	
475	  void main() {
476	    vec2 uv = vUv;
477	    float aspect = uResolution.x / uResolution.y;
478	    float f = field(uv, aspect);
479

483	                    * (1.0 - smoothstep(THRESHOLD * 0.95, THRESHOLD, f));

541	    gl_FragColor = vec4(color, alpha);
542	  }

545	// ─── MERCURY FRAGMENT SHADER ───────────────────
546	// Liquid metal — physically based metallic reflection.
547	//
548	// Mercury complex refractive index at 589nm: n = 1.62, k = 5.76
549	// Normal incidence reflectance: R = ((n-1)² + k²) / ((n+1)² + k²) ≈ 0.84
550	//
551	// Spectral reflectance (measured):
552	//   400nm (blue):  R ≈ 0.85
553	//   550nm (green): R ≈ 0.82
554	//   700nm (red):   R ≈ 0.78
555	// This gives mercury its characteristic cool steel-silver appearance.
556	//
557	// Mercury is completely opaque — extinction depth is ~15nm.
558	// No light transmits through it at any thickness.
559	// Surface tension is extremely high (485 mN/m), giving mirror-smooth surfaces.
560	
561	const fragmentShaderMercury = /* glsl */ `
562	  precision highp float;
563	  varying vec2 vUv;
564	
565	  uniform vec2 uResolution;
566	  uniform sampler2D uContentTex;
567	  uniform sampler2D uEnvMap;
568	  uniform vec3 uDroplets[${MAX_DROPLETS}];
569	  uniform vec2 uLightPos;
570	
571	  const float THRESHOLD = 0.45;
572	  // Per-channel R₀ from measured spectral reflectance of mercury
573	  const vec3 R0 = vec3(0.78, 0.82, 0.85);
574	
575	  float field(vec2 p, float aspect) {
576	    float sum = 0.0;
577	    for (int i = 0; i < ${MAX_DROPLETS}; i++) {
578	      vec2 centre = uDroplets[i].xy;
579	      float radius = uDroplets[i].z;
580	      if (radius < 0.001) continue;
581	      vec2 diff = p - centre;
582	      diff.x *= aspect;
583	      sum += exp(-dot(diff, diff) / (2.0 * radius * radius));
584	    }
585	    return sum;
586	  }
587	
588	  vec3 getNormal(vec2 p, float f, float aspect) {
589	    float e = 0.004;
590	    float l = field(p - vec2(e, 0.0), aspect);
591	    float r = field(p + vec2(e, 0.0), aspect);
592	    float d = field(p - vec2(0.0, e), aspect);
593	    float u = field(p + vec2(0.0, e), aspect);
594	    float distFromEdge = max(f - THRESHOLD, 0.0);
595	    float meniscus = exp(-distFromEdge * 8.0);
596	    return normalize(vec3((l - r) * meniscus, (d - u) * meniscus, e * 55.0));
597	  }
598	
599	  vec3 envMap(vec3 dir) {
600	    float phi   = atan(dir.z, dir.x);
601	    float theta = asin(clamp(dir.y, -1.0, 1.0));
602	    vec2 envUV  = vec2(phi / 6.28318 + 0.5, theta / 3.14159 + 0.5);
603	    vec3 hdr    = texture2D(uEnvMap, envUV).rgb;
604	    hdr *= 2.5;
605	    return hdr / (hdr + 1.0);
606	  }
607	
608	  // Schlick Fresnel for metals — per-channel R₀, power ~3 for conductors
609	  vec3 fresnelMetal(float NdV) {
610	    return R0 + (1.0 - R0) * pow(1.0 - NdV, 3.0);
611	  }
612	
613	  void main() {
614	    vec2 uv = vUv;
615	    float aspect = uResolution.x / uResolution.y;
616	    float f = field(uv, aspect);
617	
618	    // Outside: bright silver caustic at contact line
619	    if (f < THRESHOLD) {
620	      float caustic = smoothstep(THRESHOLD * 0.75, THRESHOLD * 0.95, f)
621	                    * (1.0 - smoothstep(THRESHOLD * 0.95, THRESHOLD, f));
622	      gl_FragColor = vec4(vec3(0.92), caustic * 0.15);
623	      return;
624	    }
625	
626	    vec3 N = getNormal(uv, f, aspect);
627	    float distFromEdge = max(f - THRESHOLD, 0.0);
628	    float meniscus = exp(-distFromEdge * 8.0);
629	    // Simulate perspective view — viewer at finite distance above the surface
630	    // so different points on the mercury reflect different parts of the environment
631	    vec3 V = normalize(vec3((uv - 0.5) * vec2(aspect, 1.0) * 0.7, -1.0));
632	    float NdV = max(dot(N, -V), 0.0);
633	
634	    // Spectral Fresnel — mercury is 78-85% reflective even at normal incidence
635	    vec3 F = fresnelMetal(NdV);
636	
637	    vec3 R = reflect(V, N);
638	    vec3 envColor = envMap(R);
639	
640	    // Desaturate the environment to get neutral silver — mercury's flat spectral
641	    // reflectance (0.78-0.85) means it reflects all wavelengths nearly equally,
642	    // so coloured surroundings appear muted on its surface
643	    float envLuma = dot(envColor, vec3(0.2126, 0.7152, 0.0722));
644	    envColor = mix(vec3(envLuma), envColor, 0.25);
645	
646	    // Mercury is completely opaque — colour comes entirely from reflection
647	    vec3 color = envColor * F;
648	
649	    // Specular highlights — with perspective view direction
650	    vec3 L = normalize(vec3((uLightPos - uv) * vec2(aspect, 1.0), 0.85));
651	    vec3 H = normalize(L - V);
652	    float NdH = max(dot(N, H), 0.0);
653	    // GGX-like distribution: very tight core + broader meniscus lobe
654	    float specMirror = pow(NdH, 1024.0);
655	    float specBroad  = pow(NdH, 80.0) * meniscus;
656	    color += vec3(1.0) * specMirror * 0.7;
657	    color += vec3(1.0) * specBroad * 0.06;
658	
659	    float alpha = smoothstep(THRESHOLD, THRESHOLD + 0.045, f);
660	    gl_FragColor = vec4(color, alpha);
661	  }
662	`;
663	
664	// ─── FROSTED GLASS FRAGMENT SHADER ─────────────
665	//
666	// Ground glass: surface roughened by sandblasting or acid etching.
667	// The micro-faceted surface acts as a random phase screen.
668	//
669	// Physics:
670	// - Each surface point has a random normal → transmitted light is scattered
671	// - The point-spread function is Gaussian (CLT over many micro-facets)
672	// - Angular spread σ ≈ RMS_slope × (n-1)/n for glass n ≈ 1.5
673	// - For a volume scatterer (thick frosted region), blur ∝ √(depth)
674	//   because angular deviation follows a random walk
675	// - Scattering is wavelength-independent (roughness ~1-100μm >> λ visible)
676	//   so there is NO chromatic aberration — pure luminance blur
677	// - Fresnel R₀ = ((1.5-1)/(1.5+1))² ≈ 0.04, but the rough surface
678	//   distributes both reflection and transmission into broad lobes
679	// - Forward scattering dominates: the glass is translucent, not opaque
680	// - Mie scattering from micro-features adds a slight white veil
681	//
682	// Sampling: 37-tap golden-angle spiral with Gaussian weighting for
683	// physically correct PSF approximation.
684	
685	const fragmentShaderFrosted = /* glsl */ `
686	  precision highp float;
687	  varying vec2 vUv;
688	
689	  uniform vec2 uResolution;
690	  uniform sampler2D uContentTex;
691	  uniform sampler2D uEnvMap;
692	  uniform vec3 uDroplets[${MAX_DROPLETS}];
693	  uniform vec2 uLightPos;
694	
695	  const float THRESHOLD = 0.45;
696	  const float R0 = 0.04;  // glass n ≈ 1.5
697	  const float PI = 3.14159265;
698	  const float GOLDEN_ANGLE = 2.39996323;  // π(3 - √5)
699	  const int BLUR_SAMPLES = 37;
700	
701	  float field(vec2 p, float aspect) {
702	    float sum = 0.0;
703	    for (int i = 0; i < ${MAX_DROPLETS}; i++) {
704	      vec2 centre = uDroplets[i].xy;
705	      float radius = uDroplets[i].z;
706	      if (radius < 0.001) continue;
707	      vec2 diff = p - centre;
708	      diff.x *= aspect;
709	      sum += exp(-dot(diff, diff) / (2.0 * radius * radius));
710	    }
711	    return sum;
712	  }
713	
714	  vec3 getNormal(vec2 p, float f, float aspect) {
715	    float e = 0.004;
716	    float l = field(p - vec2(e, 0.0), aspect);
717	    float r = field(p + vec2(e, 0.0), aspect);
718	    float d = field(p - vec2(0.0, e), aspect);
719	    float u = field(p + vec2(0.0, e), aspect);
720	    float distFromEdge = max(f - THRESHOLD, 0.0);
721	    float meniscus = exp(-distFromEdge * 8.0);
722	    return normalize(vec3((l - r) * meniscus, (d - u) * meniscus, e * 55.0));
723	  }
724	
725	  vec3 envMap(vec3 dir) {
726	    float phi   = atan(dir.z, dir.x);
727	    float theta = asin(clamp(dir.y, -1.0, 1.0));
728	    vec2 envUV  = vec2(phi / 6.28318 + 0.5, theta / 3.14159 + 0.5);
729	    vec3 hdr    = texture2D(uEnvMap, envUV).rgb;
730	    hdr *= 1.5;
731	    return hdr / (hdr + 1.0);
732	  }
733	
734	  // Gaussian-weighted golden-angle spiral blur
735	  // Physically models the PSF of a rough surface scatterer
736	  vec3 gaussianDiscBlur(vec2 centre, float sigma) {
737	    vec2 px = 1.0 / uResolution;
738	    vec3 totalColor = vec3(0.0);
739	    float totalWeight = 0.0;
740	
741	    for (int i = 0; i < BLUR_SAMPLES; i++) {
742	      float fi = float(i);
743	      // Golden-angle spiral: r = √(i/N) gives uniform disc distribution
744	      float r = sqrt(fi / float(BLUR_SAMPLES));
745	      float theta = fi * GOLDEN_ANGLE;
746	      vec2 offset = vec2(cos(theta), sin(theta)) * r * sigma;
747	
748	      // Gaussian weight: w = exp(-r² / 2) — heavier at centre
749	      float w = exp(-r * r * 0.5);
750	
751	      vec2 sampleUV = clamp(centre + offset * px, 0.0, 1.0);
752	      totalColor += texture2D(uContentTex, sampleUV).rgb * w;
753	      totalWeight += w;
754	    }
755	    return totalColor / totalWeight;
756	  }
757	
758	  void main() {
759	    vec2 uv = vUv;
760	    float aspect = uResolution.x / uResolution.y;
761	    float f = field(uv, aspect);
762	
763	    // Outside: soft scattered light at contact line
764	    if (f < THRESHOLD) {
765	      float caustic = smoothstep(THRESHOLD * 0.7, THRESHOLD * 0.95, f)
766	                    * (1.0 - smoothstep(THRESHOLD * 0.95, THRESHOLD, f));
767	      gl_FragColor = vec4(vec3(1.0), caustic * 0.05);
768	      return;
769	    }
770	
771	    vec3 N = getNormal(uv, f, aspect);
772	    float distFromEdge = max(f - THRESHOLD, 0.0);
773	    float meniscus = exp(-distFromEdge * 8.0);
774	
775	    // Depth into the frosted volume (for random-walk blur scaling)
776	    float depth = smoothstep(THRESHOLD, THRESHOLD + 0.25, f);
777	
778	    // Blur sigma ∝ √(depth) — random walk in angle space
779	    // Plus a meniscus contribution (edge refraction still scatters)
780	    float sigma = sqrt(depth) * 40.0 + meniscus * 6.0;
781	
782	    // Slight macroscopic refraction at the meniscus
783	    float refractionStrength = 0.008 * meniscus;
784	    vec2 refractedUV = clamp(uv + N.xy * refractionStrength, 0.0, 1.0);
785	
786	    // Gaussian-blurred content — no chromatic split (roughness >> λ)
787	    vec3 contentColor = gaussianDiscBlur(refractedUV, sigma);
788	
789	    // Mie scattering veil: micro-features scatter a fraction of light
790	    // isotropically, adding a white haze proportional to depth
791	    float veil = depth * 0.3 + meniscus * 0.08;
792	    contentColor = mix(contentColor, vec3(1.0), veil);
793	
794	    // Perspective view direction
795	    vec3 V = normalize(vec3((uv - 0.5) * vec2(aspect, 1.0) * 0.7, -1.0));
796	
797	    // Fresnel reflection — also scattered by the rough surface
798	    float NdV = max(dot(N, -V), 0.0);
799	    float fresnel = R0 + (1.0 - R0) * pow(1.0 - NdV, 5.0);
```

```text
1	/**
2	 * Export script — renders slides to PNG using Playwright.
3	 *
4	 * Usage:
5	 *   npm run export                    — export with current config
6	 *   npm run export -- --out=./output  — custom output directory
7	 *   npm run export -- --width=1920    — custom width (height = width / (16/9))
8	 */
9	
10	import { chromium } from 'playwright';
11	import { mkdir } from 'fs/promises';
12	import { resolve, join } from 'path';
13	import { fileURLToPath } from 'url';
14	
15	const __dirname = fileURLToPath(new URL('.', import.meta.url));
16	const PROJECT_ROOT = resolve(__dirname, '..');
17	
18	// Parse CLI args
19	const args = Object.fromEntries(
20	  process.argv.slice(2)
21	    .filter(a => a.startsWith('--'))
22	    .map(a => {
23	      const [k, v] = a.slice(2).split('=');
24	      return [k, v || 'true'];
25	    })
26	);
27	
28	const OUTPUT_DIR = resolve(PROJECT_ROOT, args.out || 'output');
29	const WIDTH = parseInt(args.width || '1920', 10);
30	const HEIGHT = Math.round(WIDTH / (16 / 9));
31	const DEV_SERVER = args.url || 'http://localhost:5173';
32	
33	async function main() {
34	  await mkdir(OUTPUT_DIR, { recursive: true });
35	
36	  console.log(`\nExporting slides at ${WIDTH}x${HEIGHT}`);
37	  console.log(`Output: ${OUTPUT_DIR}\n`);
38	
39	  const browser = await chromium.launch();
40	  const page = await browser.newPage({
41	    viewport: { width: WIDTH, height: HEIGHT },
42	    deviceScaleFactor: 2,  // 2x for crisp output
43	  });
44	
45	  await page.goto(DEV_SERVER, { waitUntil: 'networkidle' });
46	
47	  // Wait for fonts to load
48	  await page.evaluate(() => document.fonts.ready);
49	  // Extra settle time for font rendering
50	  await page.waitForTimeout(500);
51	
52	  // Find all slides
53	  const slideIds = await page.$$eval('.slide', els => els.map(el => el.id));
54	  console.log(`Found ${slideIds.length} slides\n`);
55	
56	  for (const id of slideIds) {
57	    const slideEl = page.locator(`#${id}`);
58	    const filename = `${id}.png`;
59	    const filepath = join(OUTPUT_DIR, filename);
60	
61	    await slideEl.screenshot({ path: filepath });
62	    console.log(`  Exported: ${filename}`);
63	  }
64	
65	  await browser.close();
66	  console.log(`\nDone. ${slideIds.length} slides exported to ${OUTPUT_DIR}\n`);
67	}
68	
69	main().catch(err => {
70	  console.error('Export failed:', err);
71	  process.exit(1);
72	});
73
```
