Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive view of the codebase. Let me compile the detailed summary for the user.

## COMPREHENSIVE DECK-GENERATOR PROJECT ANALYSIS

### 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW
This is a **presentation deck generator** built with Vite (bundler) and vanilla JavaScript, designed for creating, editing, and exporting professional presentations with multiple design themes. The tool generates slides from JSON content files, applies design tokens (color palettes and typography), renders them in the browser for editing, and exports as PNG, PDF, and PowerPoint (PPTX) files.

**Tech Stack:**
- Frontend: Vanilla JS (ES modules), HTML/CSS, Three.js (WebGL)
- Build: Vite 6.0.0
- Rendering/Export: html2canvas, Playwright (headless Chrome)
- Media: sharp (PNG optimization), pdfkit, pptxgenjs
- Compression: pako (deflate/inflate)

---

### 2. ARCHITECTURE

#### **Entry Point: index.html**
Located at `/Users/stugrey/Documents/Student Voice ICD/Conferences:Webinars/Teesside Keynote/deck-generator/index.html`

- Loads 9 pre-built token CSS files (design themes): monzo, dia, beyond-trends, artsy, ace-tate, glide, space10, superlist, wetransfer
- Google Fonts preload for 9 typography families
- Two main containers: `#controls` (UI panel) and `#deck` (slide rendering)
- Loads `/src/main.js` as module entry point

#### **Core Source Files: `/src/`**

**main.js** (954 lines) — Application orchestrator
- Initializes config/content from JSON, manages UI lifecycle
- Renders controls panel (palette/typography/shapes/pinstripes/noise selectors + Save/Export buttons)
- Implements slide pagination (5 slides per page)
- Handles slide editing (collapsible per-slide edit panels with live preview)
- Manages slide operations: duplicate, export PNG, edit inline
- Implements full export workflow (16:9 + 4:3 aspect ratios)

**layouts.js** (254 lines) — Layout template functions
- 10 layout templates organized by slide type:
  - **Cover** (2): `cover-bold-full` (Monzo-inspired, bold title), `cover-dark-minimal` (Glide-inspired, minimal)
  - **Section Divider** (2): `divider-color-burst`, `divider-dark-cinematic`
  - **Content** (2): `content-split-cards` (50/50 text + cards), `content-wide-visual` (30/70 text + grid)
  - **Data** (2): `data-big-numbers` (4 stat blocks), `data-full-bleed` (full-width stats)
  - **Statement** (2): `statement-centered` (Superlist), `statement-editorial` (WeTransfer)
- All layouts use CSS custom properties (tokens) — zero hard-coded colors/fonts
- Returns HTML strings; support regex-based text highlighting

**shapes.js** (214 lines) — Visual overlay generators
- **Simple shapes** (return HTML string):
  - `shapesNone()` — no overlay
  - `shapesPills()` — WeTransfer-style capsules
  - `shapesBlobs()` — Ace & Tate organic SVG blobs
  - `shapesBlocks()` — Artsy color rectangles
  - `shapesGoldenRect()` — DIA geometric SVG curves
- **WebGL shapes** (return `{html, init}` object):
  - 7 variants of water droplets with different materials: base, tinted (mint/coral), crude oil (iridescent), mercury (liquid metal), frosted (blurred glass), ice (crystalline)
  - Each captures slide content as texture and refracts it through metaball field

**shapes-droplets.js** (860+ lines) — Advanced WebGL material system
- Implements Three.js-based water physics with:
  - Metaball field (Gaussian kernel) for organic droplet shapes
  - Refraction through meniscus (curved water-air interface)
  - Chromatic aberration (wavelength-dependent bending)
  - Fresnel reflection (angle-dependent reflectivity)
  - Specular highlights (mirror + broad lobe)
  - Environment map lighting (HDRP from `/public/env-theater.hdr`)
- **6 shader variants**:
  1. Clear water (all optics, Fresnel R₀=0.02)
  2. Tinted water (Beer-Lambert absorption by colour)
  3. Crude oil (dark opaque, thin-film iridescence, R₀=0.04)
  4. Mercury (liquid metal, spectral reflectance 0.78-0.85 per channel, completely opaque)
  5. Frosted glass (37-tap Gaussian blur, scattering veil)
  6. Frozen ice (blue tint, internal cracks — not fully shown in excerpt)
- Droplet positions generated via seeded Mulberry32 PRNG (3–5 puddle groups + scattered drops, up to 96 total)
- Captures slide HTML as texture via html2canvas, applies refraction in real-time

**style.css** (1000+ lines) — Layout, controls, and typography
- Dark theme for editor UI (#1a1a1a background, cyan accents #5DD4E0)
- Responsive controls panel (sticky, collapsible, dark monospace styling)
- Pagination UI with prev/next buttons
- Slide container: 16:9 aspect ratio (via CSS custom property `--export-aspect`, overridable to 4:3)
- Detailed layout-specific styles for each of the 10 layouts:
  - Cover slides: title centering, pill-shaped date badges
  - Content slides: two-column layout, card grids, evidence cards
  - Data slides: stat blocks with adaptive text color/sizing (JS-driven)
  - Statement slides: centered large text with highlight spans
- Adaptive stat block contrast system (luminance-based text color selection)
- Edit panel styling (inline form grid, label/input pairs)

---

### 3. CONTENT & CONFIGURATION STRUCTURE

#### **content.json** (current, 64 slides, 2.5KB excerpt → ~43KB total)
Structure for each slide:
```json
{
  "type": "cover|section-divider|content|data|statement",
  "section": "section name",
  "title/heading": "text",
  "subtitle/body": "text",
  "highlight": "text to highlight (statement slides)",
  "attribution": "optional",
  "points": [{"title": "...", "description": "..."}, ...],  // content slides
  "stats": [{"value": "...", "label": "...", "year": "..."}, ...],  // data slides
  "author": "Dr Stuart Grey",
  "badge": "event/series",
  "date": "April 15th 2026"
}
```

**Current keynote topic:** "Who Will Control the AI Infrastructure of Higher Education?" — 64-slide presentation exploring:
- Section 1: From tool to substrate (AI moving from visible tools → invisible infrastructure)
- Section 2: The build-out (speculative capital creating real capacity)
- Section 3: Who sets the defaults? (market concentration, agenda-setting)
- Section 4: What dependence does (pedagogy, labour, data, inequality)
- Section 5: The lifecycle of capture (generosity → squeeze → lock-in)
- Section 6: A different strategy (engaged stewardship)
- Section 7: What the next decade looks like (two futures)

#### **config.json** (current)
```json
{
  "palette": "monzo",               // one of 9 color themes
  "typography": "monzo",            // one of 9 typeface sets
  "shapes": "none",                 // global default shape type
  "pinstripes": "diagonal",         // texture overlay: none|vertical|horizontal|diagonal
  "noise": 5,                       // noise opacity 0-30%
  "layouts": {                      // per-slide-type layout choice
    "cover": "cover-bold-full",
    "section-divider": "divider-color-burst",
    "content": "content-split-cards",
    "data": "data-big-numbers",
    "statement": "statement-centered"
  },
  "slideShapes": {                  // per-slide overrides (0-indexed)
    "0": "droplets",
    "3": "droplets",
    "4": "droplets-mercury",
    "5": "droplets-oil"
  }
}
```

---

### 4. DESIGN SYSTEM — TOKENS

**9 Pre-built Design Palettes** in `/tokens/`, each defining:
- **Palette** (primary/secondary/dark/accent colors, text colors)
- **Typography** (display/body fonts, sizes, weights, spacing, transforms)

**Example: Monzo theme** (`/tokens/monzo.css`)
- Colors: hot coral (#FF5C57), mint (#C8F0D8), navy (#1B2A3D)
- Fonts: Plus Jakarta Sans (display, 800wt), Inter (body, 400wt)
- Headings: 9rem max, 0.95 line-height, -0.03em tracking (tight)

**Example: WeTransfer theme** (`/tokens/wetransfer.css`)
- Colors: black (#000000), pastels (lavender #C8A8E0, lime #C8E050, periwinkle #88A8E8)
- Fonts: Playfair Display (serif, 900wt), Source Serif 4 (body)
- Headings: 6.5rem, 1.1 line-height (more open than Monzo)

Other themes: DIA (light serif, all-caps), Beyond Trends (soft rounded serif), Artsy (geometric sans), Ace & Tate (Didone serif), Glide (geometric sans, tight), SPACE10 (light clean sans), Superlist (bold geometric sans)

---

### 5. WORKFLOW & FEATURES

#### **Interactive Controls Panel**
Users can:
1. **Choose global design**: Palette (9), Typography (9), Shapes (11), Pinstripes (4), Noise (0-30%)
2. **Choose per-slide-type layouts**: 2 options each for cover, divider, content, data, statement
3. **Per-slide shape override**: Dropdown to apply different shape to individual slides
4. **Randomise**: One-click random theme combo (palette + typography + shapes + pinstripes + noise + layouts)
5. **Edit slide content**: Inline editor for all text fields and nested arrays (points, stats)
6. **Pagination**: 5 slides per page, prev/next navigation
7. **Save**: Saves content.json + config.json to disk with timestamped backups
8. **Export**: All slides to PNG, PDF, PPTX (both 16:9 and 4:3)

#### **Slide Operations** (per-slide buttons)
- **Edit**: Toggle collapsible inline editor
- **Duplicate**: Clone slide (preserves slide-specific shape overrides with index shifting)
- **Export PNG**: Download single slide as PNG

#### **Export Workflow** (`exportAllSlides()`)
1. Iterate through all slides across all pages
2. Render each slide in both 16:9 and 4:3 aspect ratios
3. Capture via html2canvas at 3840px width (2x device pixel ratio)
4. POST to `/api/save-slide` endpoint per slide
5. Optimize PNGs via sharp (lossless compression, level 9)
6. Build PPTX and PDF from PNG files via POST to `/api/build-deck`
7. Display results (64 slides exported, PPTX + PDF created)

#### **Save System**
- Two endpoints: `/api/save-content` (legacy), `/api/save` (combined)
- Auto-backs up content.json and config.json with timestamp (e.g., `content_2026-03-30T10-37-12.json`)
- Client-side dirty state tracking (Save button enabled only when changes made)

---

### 6. BUILD & DEPLOYMENT

#### **Vite Configuration** (`vite.config.js`)
- **Dev server** opens browser automatically
- **Custom middleware** for three key endpoints:
  - `/api/save`: Accepts JSON (content + config), backs up originals, writes new versions
  - `/api/save-slide`: Accepts base64 PNG data, saves to `export/{subdir}/` (16x9 or 4x3)
  - `/api/build-deck`: Iterates over exported PNGs, builds PPTX and PDF files

**PPTX/PDF generation details:**
- **PPTX**: Custom layout (16:9 = 13.333"×7.5", 4:3 = 10"×7.5"), each slide = one image
- **PDF**: PDFKit with matching dimensions, one slide per page
- Both stored in `/export/{16x9|4x3}/presentation.{pptx|pdf}`

#### **npm Scripts**
```
npm run dev        → Start Vite dev server (localhost:5173), auto-open browser
npm run build      → Vite production build (not used for this workflow)
npm run preview    → Preview production build
npm run export     → Node script using Playwright to render slides to PNG
```

**Export script** (`scripts/export.js`):
- Uses Playwright chromium headless browser
- Navigates to dev server, waits for fonts + stabilization
- Selects all `.slide` elements, screenshots each to output directory
- Configurable: `--out`, `--width`, `--url`

---

### 7. INTERACTIVE FEATURES

#### **Real-time Editing**
- Inline edit forms for slide content (title, heading, body, stats, points, etc.)
- Changes immediately re-render the slide (no page reload)
- Edit panel collapsible per-slide
- Nested arrays (points in content slides, stats in data slides) fully editable

#### **Theme Mixing**
- Palette + Typography independently selectable (e.g., Monzo colors + DIA typography)
- Pinstripes overlay (vertical/horizontal/diagonal lines via CSS or canvas texture)
- Noise texture (procedurally generated grayscale noise, opacity 0-30%)
- Per-slide shape overrides (can use different material for each slide while global default applies to rest)

#### **WebGL Interactivity** (for droplet shapes)
- Real-time 3D rendering on each slide containing droplets
- Captures slide content as texture (via html2canvas before WebGL init)
- Refracts texture through metaball field with physics-based materials
- No user interaction controls; purely visual overlays

#### **Adaptive Stat Sizing**
- `fitStatSizes()`: Measures longest stat value text, scales all blocks to match
- `fixStatBlockContrast()`: Analyzes stat block background luminance, selects black or white text adaptively
- Handles contrast borders if block color is too close to slide background

#### **Aspect Ratio Toggle**
- CSS custom property `--export-aspect` overridable via JS (default 16:9)
- 4:3 export involves full re-render of all droplets to capture content at new aspect ratio
- Fallback plain shape rendering (pills, blobs, blocks, golden-rect) works at any aspect

---

### 8. DATA PERSISTENCE & BACKUPS

All changes backed up with timestamps:
- Content changes: `content_YYYY-MM-DDTHH-mm-ss.json`
- Config changes: `config_YYYY-MM-DDTHH-mm-ss.json`
- Originals: `content.json`, `config.json` (current versions)
- Export folder: `export/{16x9|4x3}/slide-NN-{type}.png`, `.pptx`, `.pdf`

**Current backup count**: 17+ timestamped versions visible (March 30, 2026)

---

### 9. KEY TECHNICAL INNOVATIONS

1. **Physics-based WebGL droplets**: Real refraction, chromatic aberration, Fresnel, specular reflection, environment mapping — 6 distinct material presets
2. **Seeded procedural droplet generation**: Deterministic per-slide (Mulberry32 PRNG), replicable layouts
3. **Texture capture for refraction**: html2canvas snapshot embedded as WebGL content texture
4. **Adaptive typography**: Stat values auto-scale to fit blocks; text color adapts to background luminance
5. **Design token system**: CSS custom properties enable swapping entire visual identity without code changes
6. **Dual-format export**: 16:9 and 4:3 renders in single workflow
7. **Live inline editing with preview**: No modal dialogs, full re-render on keystroke
8. **Per-element shape overrides**: Can apply different materials to individual slides while maintaining global defaults

---

### 10. PRESENTATION CONTENT SUMMARY

The current keynote ("Who Will Control the AI Infrastructure of Higher Education?") uses all 10 layout types across 64 slides:
- **1 cover slide** with speaker/date badge
- **7 section dividers** (one intro section + 6 main sections)
- **40+ content/data slides** mixing two-column evidence cards, stat blocks, and text blocks
- **9+ statement slides** emphasizing key themes (Transparency, Distortion, Extraction, Control, etc.)

Themes explored:
- AI as infrastructure (not tool)
- Market concentration & dependency
- Agenda-setting and mission drift
- Pedagogical and labour impacts
- Lock-in through convenience
- Strategic alternatives (stewardship, collaboration, data governance)
- Two possible futures (capability vs. rent extraction)

---

### CONCLUSION

This is a **sophisticated presentation tool** combining:
- Modular layout + shape system (10 layouts × 11 shape types = 110 unique combinations)
- 9 design themes (palette + typography) = ~1000 total theme variations
- Real-time editing with live preview
- Enterprise-grade export (PNG + PDF + PPTX, both 16:9 and 4:3)
- Advanced WebGL material effects (water, oil, mercury, frosted glass)
- Token-driven design (fully themeable via CSS custom properties)
- Automatic backup and versioning
- Procedurally generated organic elements (droplet positions, noise textures)

The codebase is well-organized, modular, and deeply customizable. Every visual aspect can be modified through config.json or live controls without touching code.

Here's a comprehensive breakdown of the deck generator:

---

## Project Overview

A **vanilla JS + Vite presentation tool** that generates slides from JSON content, applies design tokens, renders them in-browser for live editing, and exports to PNG/PDF/PPTX. No framework — just ES modules, CSS custom properties, and some serious WebGL.

---

## The Presentation Content

**Keynote:** "Who Will Control the AI Infrastructure of Higher Education?"
**Speaker:** Dr Stuart Grey | **Date:** April 15th 2026 | **64 slides**

Seven sections:
1. **From tool to substrate** — AI moving from visible tools to invisible infrastructure
2. **The build-out** — speculative capital creating real capacity
3. **Who sets the defaults?** — market concentration, agenda-setting
4. **What dependence does** — pedagogy, labour, data, inequality impacts
5. **The lifecycle of capture** — generosity → squeeze → lock-in
6. **A different strategy** — engaged stewardship
7. **What the next decade looks like** — two futures

Uses all 5 slide types: cover, section-divider, content, data, statement.

---

## Architecture (4 core source files)

| File | Lines | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `src/main.js` | ~954 | App orchestrator: controls UI, pagination, editing, save/export |
| `src/layouts.js` | ~254 | 10 layout templates (2 per slide type), returns HTML strings |
| `src/shapes.js` | ~214 | Visual overlays: pills, blobs, blocks, golden-rect, + 7 WebGL droplet variants |
| `src/shapes-droplets.js` | ~860 | Three.js metaball physics: refraction, chromatic aberration, Fresnel, specular highlights |
| `src/style.css` | ~1000 | Dark editor UI, all layout-specific styles, adaptive stat sizing |

---

## Design System — 9 Themes in `/tokens/`

Each CSS file defines palette + typography via custom properties:
- **monzo** — hot coral, mint, navy; Plus Jakarta Sans bold
- **wetransfer** — black + pastels; Playfair Display serif
- **dia** — light serif, all-caps geometric
- **beyond-trends** — soft rounded serif
- **artsy** — geometric sans + color blocks
- **ace-tate** — Didone serif + organic blobs
- **glide** — geometric sans, tight spacing
- **space10** — light clean sans
- **superlist** — bold geometric sans

Palette and typography are **independently mixable** (e.g. Monzo colors + DIA type).

---

## Content & Config Structure

**content.json** — array of 64 slide objects:
```json
{ "type": "content", "section": "...", "heading": "...", "body": "...",
  "points": [{"title":"...", "description":"..."}], "stats": [...] }
```

**config.json** — global design choices + per-slide overrides:
```json
{ "palette": "monzo", "typography": "monzo", "shapes": "none",
  "pinstripes": "diagonal", "noise": 5,
  "layouts": { "cover": "cover-bold-full", ... },
  "slideShapes": { "0": "droplets", "4": "droplets-mercury" } }
```

---

## Shape System — 11 options

- **Simple HTML**: none, pills, blobs, blocks, golden-rect
- **WebGL droplets** (7 variants): clear water, tinted mint, tinted coral, crude oil (iridescent), mercury (liquid metal), frosted glass, ice (crystalline)

WebGL droplets capture the slide content as a texture via html2canvas, then refract it through a metaball field with physics-based materials. Droplet positions are seeded per-slide via Mulberry32 PRNG.

---

## Workflow

```
npm run dev       → Vite dev server at localhost:5173 (auto-opens browser)
npm run export    → Playwright headless screenshots (scripts/export.js)
```

**In-browser controls panel:**
1. Pick palette, typography, shapes, pinstripes, noise globally
2. Pick layout variant per slide type (2 choices each)
3. Override shapes per individual slide
4. Randomise everything in one click
5. Edit any slide's content inline (live re-render)
6. Duplicate or export individual slides
7. Save (writes content.json + config.json with timestamped backups)
8. Export all — renders every slide at 3840px in both 16:9 and 4:3, then builds PPTX + PDF

**Export pipeline** (via Vite middleware in `vite.config.js`):
- Each slide → html2canvas → base64 PNG → POST `/api/save-slide` → sharp optimization
- Then POST `/api/build-deck` → pptxgenjs + pdfkit → `export/{16x9|4x3}/presentation.{pptx,pdf}`

---

## Backup System

Every save creates timestamped copies (e.g. `content_2026-03-30T10-37-12.json`). There are 17+ backups from March 30 development. Current working versions are always `content.json` and `config.json`.

---

## Key Technical Highlights

- **Token-driven**: swap entire visual identity by changing one CSS file
- **110 layout×shape combos** per slide, ~1000+ theme variations total
- **Physics-based WebGL**: real Fresnel reflection, chromatic aberration, Beer-Lambert absorption, thin-film iridescence
- **Adaptive stat sizing**: auto-scales text to fit blocks, picks black/white text based on background luminance
- **Dual-aspect export**: full 16:9 and 4:3 in one workflow
- **No framework overhead**: ~2300 lines of source code total

This gives you a solid foundation for both tweaking the presentation content and evolving the tool itself. What would you like to tackle first?

195	  const text = slide.text.replace(
196	    new RegExp(`(${escapeRegex(slide.highlight)})`, 'gi'),
197	    '<span class="highlight">$1</span>'
198	  );
199	
200	  return `
201	    <div class="slide-inner statement-centered">
202	      <div class="statement-label">${slide.section || ''}</div>
203	      <div class="statement-text">${text}</div>
204	      ${slide.attribution ? `<div class="statement-attribution">${slide.attribution}</div>` : ''}
205	    </div>
206	  `;
207	}
208

211	    new RegExp(`(${escapeRegex(slide.highlight)})`, 'gi'),
212	    '<span class="highlight">$1</span>'
213	  );
214	
215	  return `
216	    <div class="slide-inner statement-editorial">
217	      <div class="statement-label">${slide.section || ''}</div>
218	      <div class="statement-text">${text}</div>
219	      ${slide.attribution ? `<div class="statement-attribution">${slide.attribution}</div>` : ''}
220	    </div>
221	  `;
222	}
223	
224	// ─── HELPERS ────────────────────────────────────
225	
226	function escapeRegex(str) {
227	  if (!str) return '';
228	  return str.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
229	}
230

**Currently** each content-split-cards slide has evidence cards like:

**After the change**, the visible card becomes just:
- **[coral dot] Teaching and assessment** (larger text, no description)

- `"Teaching and assessment: Generative tools now sit inside drafting, feedback, marking support and redesign of assessment."`
- `"Student support and admin: Copilots, triage systems and analytics are creeping into advising, helpdesks and routine services."`
- `"Research and governance: Search, synthesis, coding, grant development and management reporting increasingly run through AI-mediated layers."`

2. **`content.json`** — for all 37 content slides, append each point's `title: description` to `notes.points`, then remove `description` from each point

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-03 09:57:07Z)**_

Now the JSON transformation — I'll use a node script to move descriptions into notes for all content slides.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-03 09:57:12Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Migrate point descriptions to notes for all content slides

```bash
cd "/Users/stugrey/Documents/Student Voice ICD/Conferences:Webinars/Teesside Keynote/deck-generator" && node -e "
const fs = require('fs');
const data = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('content.json', 'utf8'));

data.slides.forEach(slide => {
  if (slide.type === 'content' && slide.points && slide.points.length > 0) {
    // Append each point's description to notes.points
    slide.points.forEach(point => {
      if (point.description) {
        slide.notes.points.push(point.title + ': ' + point.description);
        delete point.description;
      }
    });
  }
});

fs.writeFileSync('content.json', JSON.stringify(data, null, 2) + '\n');
console.log('Done');
"
```

```text
Done
```
</details></tool-use>
